DSSSB TGT Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Scorecard

Quick Summary: Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released DSSSB TGT Result 2026 (various subjects like Maths, Natural Science, Hindi, Social Science, Sanskrit etc.) in phases starting May-June 2026 at official portal dsssb.delhi.gov.in / dsssbonline.nic.in. Candidates can check marks & shortlist status using Application Number & Password/DOB. Check merit list, cutoff, and e-dossier process below.

The Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board has officially published results for multiple TGT posts (Trained Graduate Teacher) in phases. Candidates who appeared in the Tier-I exams can now check their marks, qualifying status, and e-dossier shortlisting online.

This article provides complete information about DSSSB TGT Result 2026 including direct links, subject-wise updates, merit lists, cutoffs, and step-by-step instructions.

DSSSB TGT 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB)
Post Name TGT (Maths, Natural Science, Hindi, Social Science, Sanskrit & Others)
Result Type Tier-I Marks & Shortlist for e-Dossier
Result Declaration Date May-June 2026 (Phase-wise)
Exam Conducted On 2025 (Various Dates)
Selection Stages Tier-I Exam → e-Dossier → Document Verification → Final Merit
Official Website https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in/

Check Your TGT Result Now: Download DSSSB TGT Result 2026

How to Check DSSSB TGT Result 2026?

  1. Visit the official website: https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in/ or OARS portal https://dsssbonline.nic.in/.
  2. Go to the “Latest Result” or “Results” section.
  3. Click on the relevant notice for your TGT subject/post code.
  4. Login using Application Number and Password / DOB.
  5. View marks, download scorecard/PDF, and check shortlist status.
  6. Save and print for future reference.

DSSSB TGT Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The DSSSB TGT merit list 2026 includes shortlisted candidates for e-dossier submission. Separate lists are prepared category-wise.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Number / Application Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Category (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Marks Obtained
  • Shortlist Status
  • Cutoff Details

Types of Lists: Subject-wise, Category-wise, and Overall Shortlist for e-Dossier.

DSSSB TGT Scorecard 2026 - Information Breakdown

Candidate Name As per application
Application/Roll Number Unique ID
Exam Details Tier-I Marks
Section-wise Marks Part A & Part B scores
Total Marks Out of 200
Qualifying Status Shortlisted / Not Shortlisted

Important Instructions for Shortlisted Candidates

  • Upload e-dossier within the given window (e.g., June 2026 dates vary by subject)
  • Prepare all original documents for verification
  • Check official notices regularly for updates
  • Report discrepancies immediately to DSSSB helpdesk
  • e-Dossier upload does not guarantee final selection

How to Download UP PGT Interview Letter 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your UP PGT Interview Letter 2026 :

  1. Step 1: Visit the official UPESSC website at upessc.up.gov.in or click the direct download link provided above
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the "Announcement" / "Interview Schedule" section on the homepage
  3. Step 3: Click on "UP PGT Interview Letter 2026" or subject-wise schedule link

Your UP PGT Interview Letter 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth

Mandatory Documents (Originals + Photocopies):

  • UP PGT Interview Letter 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)

    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • All Educational Certificates & Marksheets
  • Recent passport-size photographs

Not Allowed:

  • Mobile phones and electronic devices
  • Study materials or notes
  • Bags (except small ones as permitted)

Important Instructions for UP PGT Interview 2026

Before Interview Day:

  • Download call letter well in advance
  • Check venue location and plan travel
  • Arrange all required documents
  • Prepare for subject-specific questions

On Interview Day:

  • Reach venue 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Follow all instructions from officials
  • Maintain professionalism and confidence

What If UP PGT Interview Letter Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your UP PGT Interview Letter 2026 , try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser
  3. Solution 3: Verify your qualification status on official website
  4. Solution 4: Contact UPESSC helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues. Check your registered email/SMS for notifications.

UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026 Out @ uppbpb.gov.in

The UP Police Sub Inspector (SI) & Platoon Commander Recruitment 2025 (Short Notice Date: 28 March 2025) is being conducted by the Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) to fill 4,543 vacancies across four categories- Sub Inspector (Civil Police), Sub Inspector (Female), Platoon Commander, and SI / Platoon Commander (Special Security Force).

Candidates who appeared in the Written Exam on 14 - 15 March 2026 and were shortlisted in the Result declared on 07 May 2026 are now eligible for the next stage- Document Verification (DV) and Physical Standard Test (PST). The UPPRPB has officially published the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026 on 05 June 2026 at the official exam portal siupexam25.com. The DV & PST is scheduled to be conducted on 15–16 June 2026.

UP Police SI DV & PST Hall Ticket 2026 – PDF Download Link

Shortlisted candidates can download the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026 through the direct link provided below. They must enter their Enrollment Number / Registration Number or Date of Birth as login credentials. Note that no login is required to check the shortlist result PDF- only the DV & PST admit card requires candidate login.

Download Here: UP Police SI DV & PST Hall Ticket 2026

How to Download UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026 at uppbpb.gov.in?

How can I download my UP Police SI DV & PST Hall Ticket 2026?

Follow the step-by-step process below to download the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official UPPRPB exam portal- siupexam25.com or the UPPRPB website at uppbpb.gov.in.
  2. Step 2: On the homepage, click on the "Admit Card / Notifications" section.
  3. Step 3: Find and click on "UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026" or "Sub Inspector (SI) DV / PST District Intimation".
  4. Step 4: You will be redirected to the candidate login page.
  5. Step 5: Enter your Enrollment Number / Registration Number and Date of Birth as login credentials.
  6. Step 6: Click "Submit" to proceed. Your DV & PST admit card PDF will open on screen.
  7. Step 7: Verify all details- name, roll number, DV & PST date, reporting time, and venue address.
  8. Step 8: Download and take at least 2–3 clear printouts for use on the DV & PST day.

What Details Are Mentioned on the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026?

After downloading the hall ticket, candidates must carefully verify each detail. In case of any discrepancy, contact UPPRPB before the DV & PST date. The following information is printed on the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026:

  • Candidate's Full Name
  • Roll Number / Enrollment Number / Registration Number
  • Candidate's Photograph
  • Candidate's Signature
  • Father's / Mother's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Gender
  • Category
  • Post Name (SI Civil Police / Platoon Commander / SSF)
  • DV & PST Date and Reporting Time
  • DV & PST Venue (District / Centre Name and Full Address)
  • Centre Code
  • Important Instructions for DV & PST day
  • Invigilator / Verification Officer Signature Space

Stage Date Details

DV & PST Admit Card Released 05 June 2026 Available at siupexam25.com | Login required

Physical Efficiency Test (PET) To Be Announced After DV & PST clearance
Medical Test To Be Announced Final stage before appointment

Category For Male Candidates   For Female Candidates  
  Height Chest (Un-expanded / Expanded) Height Min. Weight

Category Race Distance Time Duration

Female Candidates 2.4 KM 16 Minutes

UP Police SI 2025 – Selection Process

The complete UP Police Sub Inspector 2025 selection process consists of the following five stages. Candidates must clear each stage to proceed to the next:

  1. Written Examination – Objective OMR-based test (completed on 14–15 March 2026)
  2. Document Verification (DV) – Verification of original educational and personal documents
  3. Physical Standard Test (PST) – Height, weight / chest measurement check as per category norms
  4. Physical Efficiency Test (PET) – Running race (4.8 KM for males / 2.4 KM for females)
  5. Medical Test – Final medical fitness examination before appointment

UP Police SI 2025 – Application Fee

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC ₹500/-
SC / ST ₹400/-
Payment Mode Online – Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, Mobile Wallet

Documents to Carry for UP Police SI DV & PST 2026

Candidates must carry the following original documents along with self-attested photocopies to the DV & PST centre on 15–16 June 2026. Keep everything ready 2–3 days before to avoid last-minute rush:

  • Printed copy of the UP Police SI DV & PST Admit Card 2026
  • Valid government-issued Photo ID (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Driving Licence, PAN Card)
  • Passport-sized Photograph (same as in application form)
  • Graduation Degree / Provisional Degree Certificate (original + photocopy- mandatory)
  • All Graduation Mark Sheets (original + photocopy)
  • 10th and 12th Mark Sheet & Certificate
  • Caste / Category Certificate (if applicable)- issued by competent authority
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate of Uttar Pradesh
  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwBD Certificate (if applicable)
  • NCC "B" Certificate (if applicable- preference qualification)
  • "O" Level Computer Certificate from NIELIT (if applicable- preference qualification)
  • Two-year Territorial Army Service Certificate (if applicable)

Documents NOT to Carry on UP Police SI DV & PST Day 2026

The following items are strictly prohibited inside the DV & PST centre:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, or fitness bands
  • Bluetooth devices or wireless earphones
  • Electronic gadgets of any kind
  • Bags or purses (other than document file/folder)
  • Any unauthorized printed material or notes

RBI Grade B Admit Card 2026 Out @ rbi.org.in

The RBI Grade B (Officer Grade 'B') Recruitment 2026 is conducted by the Reserve Bank of India  India's central banking institution to recruit officers across three streams: General (DR), DEPR (Department of Economic and Policy Research), and DSIM (Department of Statistics and Information Management). The official RBI Grade B Notification 2026 was released on 29 April 2026 for a total of 60 vacancies  the lowest in recent years, making competition significantly tighter than before.

As the Phase 1 examination dates of 13-14 June 2026 approach, RBI has released the Phase 1 Call Letter (Admit Card) on 05 June 2026 at opportunities.rbi.org.in. The Phase 1 exam is an online objective-type test of 200 marks and 120 minutes. Note that Phase 1 is only qualifying in nature  the final merit list is prepared solely on the basis of Phase 2 marks and Interview score.

RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026 - PDF Download Link

Candidates who have successfully registered for the RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 can download their Phase 1 call letter through the direct link provided below. Login credentials required are: Registration Number / Roll Number + Password / Date of Birth. 

RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026: Click Here to Download

RBI Grade B Phase 1 Exam Date 2026 (DEPR / DSIM) 14 June 2026 (Saturday)

Official Website opportunities.rbi.org.in

RBI Grade B 2026 - Vacancy Breakup

The RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 covers 60 posts across three specialised streams:

  • General (DR): 40 Posts
  • DEPR(Economic & Policy Research): 10 Posts
  • DSIM(Statistics & Information Management): 10 Posts

How to Download RBI Grade B Admit Card 2026 at rbi.org.in?

How can I download my RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026 from rbi.org.in?

Follow the step-by-step process below to download the RBI Grade B Phase 1 Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Open your browser and visit the official RBI website  rbi.org.in.
  2. Step 2: Click on the "Opportunities@RBI" section on the homepage.
  3. Step 3: Open the "Current Vacancies" tab and look for the Call Letters / Admit Card link.
  4. Step 4: Click on "Admission Letters for Officer in Grade B (General / DEPR / DSIM) - PY 2026".
  5. Step 5: You will be redirected to the candidate login page.
  6. Step 6: Enter your Registration Number / Roll Number and Password / Date of Birth, then complete the captcha verification.
  7. Step 7: Click "Submit". Your RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026 will appear on screen.
  8. Step 8: Verify all details carefully name, photo, exam date, shift, and exam centre address.
  9. Step 9: Download the call letter PDF and take at least 2-3 clear printouts for use on the examination day.

What Details Are Mentioned on the RBI Grade B Admit Card 2026?

After downloading the call letter, candidates must carefully check each detail. In case of any discrepancy, contact RBI authorities immediately before the exam date. The following details are printed on the RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026:

  • Candidate's Full Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Candidate's Photograph
  • Candidate's Signature
  • Date of Birth
  • Gender
  • Category
  • Post Name and Stream (General / DEPR / DSIM)
  • Phase 1 Exam Date and Reporting Time
  • Shift Timing
  • Exam Centre Name and Full Address
  • Exam Centre Code
  • Important Exam-Day Instructions
  • Invigilator / Verification Space

RBI Grade B Exam Date 2026 - Complete Schedule

Candidates can check the complete RBI Grade B 2026 examination schedule phase-wise below:

Examination Phase Stream Exam Date Details
Phase 1 (Prelims) General (DR) 13 June 2026 (Friday) Online CBT | 200 Marks | 120 Minutes | Objective Type
DEPR / DSIM 14 June 2026 (Saturday) Online CBT | 200 Marks | 120 Minutes | Objective Type
Phase 2 (Mains) General (DR) 25 July 2026 Objective + Descriptive | ESI, F&M, English Papers
DEPR / DSIM 26 July 2026 Specialised Economics (DEPR) / Statistics (DSIM) Papers
Phase 3 (Interview) All Streams To Be Announced 75 Marks | Interview + Psychometric Test

Click Here: RBI Grade B Syllabus 2026

RBI Grade B 2026 - Phase 1 Exam Pattern

Section No. of Questions Marks Duration
General Awareness Sectional 200 Total 120 Minutes
English Language Sectional
Quantitative Aptitude Sectional
Reasoning Ability Sectional
Note: Negative marking of 0.25 marks per wrong answer. Phase 1 is qualifying only  marks are not counted in the final merit list. Final merit = Phase 2 + Interview scores only.

Click Here: RBI Grade B Phase 1 Exam Pattern 2026

RBI Grade B 2026 - Selection Process

The RBI Grade B 2026 selection process consists of three stages. Candidates must clear each stage to progress to the next:

  1. Phase 1 - Preliminary Exam (Qualifying) - Online objective CBT | 200 marks | 120 minutes | Negative marking of 0.25 per wrong answer | Qualifying nature only
  2. Phase 2 - Main Exam (Merit-Based) - Objective + Descriptive papers | Papers on Economic & Social Issues (ESI), Finance & Management (F&M), and English | Specialised papers for DEPR/DSIM streams
  3. Phase 3 - Interview - 75 marks | Candidates appear for a Psychometric Test before the interview | Final merit based on Phase 2 + Interview marks

RBI Grade B 2026 - Salary & Perks

Pay Component Amount
Basic Pay ₹78,450 per month
Gross Monthly Salary (incl. all allowances) ₹1,54,936 per month
Additional Benefits HRA, Medical Coverage, Pension, Travel Allowance, Concessional Housing Loan, Staff Welfare facilities

Documents to Carry Along with RBI Grade B Hall Ticket 2026

On the day of the RBI Grade B Phase 1 examination 2026, candidates must carry the following documents to the exam centre. Keep them ready 2-3 days before the exam:

  • Printout of the RBI Grade B Phase 1 Call Letter 2026 (colour printout preferred)
  • Valid government-issued Photo ID (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Driving Licence, PAN Card, Passport)
  • Passport-sized Photograph (same as uploaded in the application form)

Documents NOT to Carry on RBI Grade B Exam Day 2026

The following items are strictly prohibited inside RBI Grade B 2026 examination centres:

  • Textbooks, notebooks, or any printed/handwritten study material
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, or fitness bands
  • Bluetooth devices or wireless earphones
  • Calculators or digital watches
  • Electronic gadgets of any kind
  • Bags, purses, or wallets
  • Geometry boxes, pencil boxes, or pouches

UP Police Constable 2026 - Selection Process

Candidates qualifying in the written examination will be called for subsequent rounds. The full UP Police Constable 2026 selection process consists of four stages:

  1. Written Examination (PBT) - Objective MCQ paper (150 questions / 300 marks / 2 hours)
  2. Document Verification (DV) - Verification of original educational and personal documents
  3. Physical Measurement Test (PMT) - Height, weight, and chest measurement check
  4. Physical Efficiency Test (PET) - Running, high jump, long jump, and other physical tasks

Documents to Carry Along with UP Police Constable Hall Ticket 2026

On the day of the UP Police Constable Written Exam 2026, candidates must carry the following documents to the exam centre. Keep them ready 2-3 days before the exam to avoid any last-minute rush:

  • Printout of the UP Police Constable Admit Card 2026 (colour printout preferred)
  • Valid Aadhaar Card (mandatory for e-KYC biometric verification at the centre)
  • Any other valid government-issued Photo ID (Voter ID, Driving Licence, PAN Card, Passport)
  • Passport-sized Photograph (same as uploaded in the application form)

Documents NOT to Carry on UP Police Constable Exam Day 2026

The following items are strictly prohibited inside UP Police Constable 2026 examination centres to ensure a fair and transparent exam process:

  • Textbooks, notebooks, or any printed/handwritten study material
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, or fitness bands
  • Bluetooth devices or wireless earphones
  • Calculators or digital watches
  • Electronic gadgets of any kind
  • Bags, purses, or wallets

  • Any electronic / communication device

  • Online Registration Deadline Extended Link

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

SBI is recruiting 7150 Apprentice posts with Any Graduate qualification, last date 15 June 2026.

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), State Bank of India
Total Training Seats 7150 (across 34 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)
Duration of Training 1 Year (On-the-Job Training)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + SBI website)
Selection Mode Online Written Test + Test of Local Language + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Online Examination Tentatively in July 2026
Official Website https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 19/05/2026
Opening of Online Registration of Application 19-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration of Application 08-06-2026
Online Registration Deadline Extended 15-06-2026
Age Calculated As On 01-04-2026
Call Letter / Acquaint Yourself Booklet Download Tentatively in June 2026
Online Written Examination Date Tentatively in July 2026
Local Language Test Date To be notified after Online Written Examination result

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 7150 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only.

Abbreviations: SC – Scheduled Caste, ST – Scheduled Tribe, OBC – Other Backward Classes, EWS – Economically Weaker Section, UR – Unreserved, VI – Visually Impaired, HI – Hearing Impaired, LD – Locomotor Disability, d&e – Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disorder, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities. Reservation for PwBD is horizontal within overall vacancies. Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description
Visual Impairment (VI) Blindness (total absence of sight / visual acuity less than 3/60 in better eye / field less than 10°) and Low Vision (visual acuity not exceeding 6/18 / field less than 40°)
Hearing Impaired (HI) Hard of Hearing – 60 DB to 70 DB hearing loss in speech frequencies in both ears
Locomotor Disability (LD) OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Muscular Dystrophy, Acid Attack Victims, Spinal Deformity (SD) and Spinal Injury (SI) without associated neurological/limb dysfunction
d & e Categories Autism Spectrum Disorder (Mild); Multiple Disabilities among LD, VI, HI, SLD, MI & ASD

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-04-2026). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online through SBI website (https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers).
Educational Qualification (as on 01-04-2026) Graduation from a recognized University / Institute.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either in SBI or in any other organization earlier, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after attainment of educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
CIBIL Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid outstanding till date of issuance of offer of engagement shall not be eligible.
Aadhaar Requirement All candidates (except those belonging to Northeastern States where alternate ID proof will do) should produce Aadhaar Number before engagement, i.e., contract signing for apprenticeship training.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-04-2026, i.e., candidates must have been born not later than 01-04-2006 and not earlier than 02-04-1998 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
PwBD As per Government of India guidelines

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for payment of fees. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
General / OBC / EWS Rs. 300/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD NIL

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on (i) Online Written Test and (ii) Test of Local Language, followed by Medical Examination. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, qualifying in local language test, and medical fitness. A Wait List (State-category wise) will be maintained for one year from the date of result or next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. Candidates will be released only once from this waitlist against non-joining subject to availability of qualified candidates.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Objective Type)

Sl. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
2 General English 25 25 15 minutes English only
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
4 Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
Total 100 100 60 minutes  

Each test has a separate timing as mentioned above. There will be NEGATIVE MARKS for wrong answers — 1/4th of the mark assigned for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer. Test questions (except General English) will be set in 13 Regional Languages viz. Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu — in addition to English and Hindi — as applicable State-wise. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order; tie-break is by age in descending order.

Medium of Examination – State/UT-wise

Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam
1 Andaman & Nicobar English, Hindi 18 Maharashtra English, Hindi, Marathi
2 Andhra Pradesh English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu 19 Manipur English, Hindi, Manipuri
3 Arunachal Pradesh English, Hindi 20 Meghalaya English, Hindi
4 Assam English, Hindi, Assamese, Bengali 21 Mizoram English, Hindi
5 Bihar English, Hindi 22 Nagaland English, Hindi
6 Chandigarh English, Hindi, Punjabi 23 Odisha English, Hindi, Odia
7 Chhattisgarh English, Hindi 24 Puducherry English, Hindi, Tamil
8 Goa English, Hindi, Konkani 25 Punjab English, Hindi, Punjabi
9 Gujarat English, Hindi, Gujarati 26 Rajasthan English, Hindi
10 Haryana English, Hindi, Punjabi 27 Sikkim English, Hindi
11 Himachal Pradesh English, Hindi 28 Tamil Nadu English, Hindi, Tamil
12 Jammu & Kashmir English, Hindi, Urdu 29 Telangana English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu
13 Jharkhand English, Hindi 30 Tripura English, Hindi, Bengali
14 Karnataka English, Hindi, Kannada 31 Uttar Pradesh English, Hindi, Urdu
15 Kerala English, Hindi, Malayalam 32 Uttarakhand English, Hindi
16 Ladakh English, Hindi, Urdu 33 West Bengal English, Hindi, Bengali
17 Madhya Pradesh English, Hindi 34 Delhi English, Hindi

Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

The test of specified local language will be conducted after qualifying the online written examination. Candidates who fail to qualify this test will not be engaged as apprentice. Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified local language will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates must appear at their own expense; SBI will not reimburse travelling expenses.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank and satisfactory completion of KYE formalities.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Weightage in Junior Associate Recruitment

Weightage/Relaxation to apprentices in recruitment of Junior Associates shall be given as per the recruitment policy of the Bank as amended from time to time, to apprentices who successfully complete the apprenticeship training in SBI, subject to candidate's satisfactory conduct and performance during the entire training period. Successful completion refers to passing of the skill evaluation test (Trade test) and obtention of the joint National Apprenticeship Certificate issued by SBI-NSDC / BFSI-SSC.

Competition Level Analysis for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high due to 7150 seats being offered at the all-India level and the exam being open to any graduate, including those already preparing for bank and SSC exams. While the written test is of easy to moderate difficulty and slightly below SBI Clerk level, the large number of serious aspirants in high-population states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will push state-wise and category-wise cut-offs upward.

In practical terms, each seat is likely to attract dozens to over a hundred candidates in major states, making speed, accuracy, and sectional balance crucial to clearing the single-stage written exam followed by the local language test. However, the effective competition reduces at later stages because some candidates will fail to qualify the language test or be filtered out at the document verification, CIBIL, Aadhaar, and medical fitness stages, and SBI will finally select candidates strictly on state-wise and category-wise merit from those who clear all stages.

Who should NOT apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job in SBI; this is only a 1‑year apprenticeship with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment.
  • Candidates who already completed any Apprenticeship (in SBI or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates outside the age band 20–28 years as on the cut-off date (even 1 day younger/older is not eligible, except notified relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
  • Aspirants who do not have a Graduation degree from a recognised University/Institute by the specified date.
  • Candidates not fluent in the notified local language of the State/UT (reading, writing, speaking, understanding), and who are unlikely to clear the language test or do not have 10th/12th proof.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register on the NAPS portal before applying, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for SBI Apprentice.
  • Candidates with serious CIBIL/loan/credit‑card defaults who have not regularised or repaid outstanding dues by the time of offer; such profiles are normally rejected in bank recruitments.
  • Ex‑Servicemen / Disabled Ex‑Servicemen, where the notification clearly states no engagement / no reservation for them under Apprentice.
  • PwBD candidates whose disability type falls under “likely to improve” (temporary disability) where reservation benefits may not apply as per notification conditions.
  • Candidates who cannot produce valid Aadhaar (except where exempted, e.g., certain North‑Eastern states with alternate ID allowed).

Common mistakes / What to check before applying SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying in a wrong State or language where you cannot comfortably read, write and speak the specified local language.
  • Entering wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed later and mismatch with certificates at DV can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer applying as OBC instead of UR; only non‑creamy OBCs with valid, recent certificate get reservation.
  • Uploading blurred/incorrect photo, signature, thumb impression or declaration (wrong size, wrong format, someone else’s handwriting) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using invalid or inactive mobile number/email ID, causing failure to receive OTPs, registration details, call letters or further communication.
  • Submitting multiple applications for different states or with different details; SBI usually accepts only the last complete application and may forfeit extra fees.
  • Ignoring negative marking and over‑attempting in the exam, which can sharply reduce your score despite high attempts.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later protesting about absorption or permanent posting after apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online; no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and read the detailed guidelines on https://bfsissc.com before applying online through the SBI website.

Candidates should have a valid email ID and mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (as per Annexure-I):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink (240×240 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) — in candidate's own handwriting
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at the time of online application)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Examination / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) along with photo identity proof (Passport / Aadhaar / PAN Card / Driving License / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Identity Card issued by School or College / Gazetted Officer in official letterhead) in original and self-attested photocopy
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST candidates) — to be submitted at the time of engagement if called for
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed Government of India format, issued during the period 01-04-2026 to the date of joining (for OBC candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in the format prescribed by Government of India, valid for the year 2026-27 (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by a Competent Authority as per Government of India guidelines (for PwBD candidates)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (Graduation onwards)

Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to SBI. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter for Online Written Examination will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own cost; SBI will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.

Apply Online at: https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

For queries: Call 022-22820427 (11:00 AM to 06:00 PM on working days) or visit https://cgrs.ibps.in — mention "Engagement of Apprentice in SBI" in the subject.

Important Instructions for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. NOR IS THE SAME CONTRACT EMPLOYMENT. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of SBI and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for employees of the Bank.
  • SBI shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to Apprentices during and/or after the completion of the Apprenticeship period. After completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.
  • Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online on SBI website is required.
  • No Ex-Servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an Apprentice and there is no reservation for Ex-Servicemen in the Apprentice Engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General or General (LD/VI/HI/d&e) as applicable.
  • Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.
  • Candidates with CIBIL default record (unpaid loans/credit card dues) are not eligible for engagement until dues are regularized/repaid.
  • All candidates (except Northeastern State candidates) must produce Aadhaar Number before engagement/contract signing.
  • Not more than one application should be submitted; in case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • PwBD candidates with disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability) category are not eligible for reservation.
  • PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility: candidates with 40% or more disability having physical limitation to write are eligible. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at his/her own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination is allowed (whether availing scribe or not) for eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their registered email ID/mobile active for receiving call letters/advices etc.
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only — candidates should visit regularly.
  • No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. No Travel Expenses will be reimbursed/paid by SBI.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.
  • Bank reserves right to cancel the selection process partly/entirely at any stage.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways have released an indicative Centralised Employment Notification (CEN No. 02/2026) for the recruitment of Technician Grade I Signal and various Technician Grade III posts. Applications will be accepted through online mode only, and a total of 6,565 Technician vacancies are expected to be filled across all RRBs for the 2026-27 recruitment cycle.

FOLLOW US

RRB Technician Notification 2026 Overview

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs)
Post Name Technician Grade I Signal & Technician Grade III
No of Posts 6,565 (Tentative)
Job Type Railway Jobs / Central Government Jobs
Pay Level (7th CPC) Technician Grade I Signal – Level 5, Technician Grade III – Level 2
Initial Pay Technician Grade I Signal – Rs. 29,200/-, Technician Grade III – Rs. 19,900/-
Medical Standard As per CEN (B-1 and other standards as notified)
Age Limit 18–36 Years (Tech. Grade I Signal), 18–33 Years (Tech. Grade III)
Opening Date of Application 30-06-2026
Closing Date for Submission of Application 29-07-2026 (23:59 hrs)
Short Notice RRB Technician Indicative Notice CEN 02/2026
Official Website https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in (and all regional RRB websites)

Important Dates

  • Indicative Notice Date: 23-05-2026
  • Opening Date of Online Application: 30-06-2026
  • Closing Date for Submission of Application: 29-07-2026 (23:59 hrs)
  • CBT Exam Schedule: 06, 09, 10 & 13 March 2026 (as per revised CBT schedule for CEN 02/2025)

RRB Technician Vacancy 2026 – Post Details

As per the approved intent, 6,565 Technician vacancies are proposed for the RRB Technician 2026-27 recruitment, distributed as follows across all RRBs. 

  • Technician Grade I Signal – 323 Posts
  • Technician Grade III – 6,242 Posts
  • Total Vacancies (All RRBs) – 6,565 Posts

Who Can Apply for RRB Technician 2026?

Eligibility criteria depend on the post and level. Candidates must meet educational, technical, and age requirements as per the Centralised Employment Notification.

  • Technician Grade I Signal: B.Sc. (Physics / Electronics / Computer Science / IT / Instrumentation) or Degree/Diploma in Engineering in relevant discipline from a recognized institution.
  • Technician Grade III: 10th / Matriculation plus ITI from NCVT/SCVT in relevant trade or Act Apprenticeship in the concerned trade.
  • Other Conditions: Candidates must satisfy prescribed medical standards and citizenship norms and should be within the specified age limit as on the date mentioned in CEN 02/2026.

Age Limit

  • Technician Grade I Signal: 18 to 36 Years
  • Technician Grade III: 18 to 33 Years
  • Upper age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/NCL/EWS/Ex-Servicemen/ PWBD) will be applicable as per Government of India rules and details will be given in the detailed CEN. 

Selection Process

RRB Technician recruitment involves multiple stages including a Computer Based Test and subsequent document and medical verification. There is no Computer Based Aptitude Test for these Technician posts.

  • Stage 1: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Stage 2: Document Verification
  • Stage 3: Medical Examination

RRB Technician Exam Pattern 2026

  • Mode of Exam: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Questions: 100 Objective Type Questions
  • Total Marks: 100 Marks
  • Exam Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: 1/3rd mark will be deducted for each wrong answer; no negative marks for unattempted questions. 

Salary Details

  • Technician Grade I Signal: Pay Level 5 (7th CPC) – Initial Pay Rs. 29,200/-
  • Technician Grade III: Pay Level 2 (7th CPC) – Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-
  • Additional allowances such as DA, HRA, TA and other benefits will be admissible as per Railway rules for the respective pay levels. 

How to Apply for RRB Technician Recruitment 2026

  • Visit your regional RRB website (e.g., Ahmedabad, Ajmer, Bengaluru, Chennai, Secunderabad, etc.).
  • Open the link for Centralised Employment Notification CEN 02/2026 – Technician.
  • Read the detailed notification carefully including post-wise eligibility, instructions and list of RRBs.
  • Register by providing basic details like name, mobile number and email ID.
  • Login using the registration credentials and fill the online application form for the desired post and pay level (only one RRB and one pay level allowed per candidate).
  • Upload required documents, photograph and signature as per specifications. >Pay the prescribed application fee through online mode, if applicable.
  • Submit the application form and take a printout / save PDF for future reference.

Important Points Mentioned in Indicative Notice

  • Each candidate can apply to only one RRB for a specific pay level and only one common online application is allowed for all notified posts in that pay level.
  • Separate applications must be submitted for each pay level, if eligible.
  • Candidates are strongly advised to verify name, date of birth and other details using Aadhaar to avoid mismatches at later stages.
  • RRBs caution candidates to be aware of touts, brokers and job racketeers and to rely only on official RRB websites for genuine information.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Combined Graduate Level Examination Notification 2026 on 21 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 12,256 Group B and Group C posts across various Ministries and Departments. Candidates within the age range as specified in the official notification and holding a Graduation Degree are eligible to apply online at https://ssc.gov.in. The application process will be active from 21 May 2026 to 22 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

SSC CGL 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Block No. 12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, has issued the official notice for Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 vide F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026.The notification includes complete information on vacancies, eligibility, age limits, reservation, examination scheme, syllabus, exam centres, application process, and candidate instructions. Applicants should read the official notice carefully before submitting the form.

  • Download SSC CGL 2026 Official Notification PDF

SSC CGL 2026 Overview

SSC is conducting Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 for approximately 12,256 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts under different Ministries, Departments, and Government of India organizations. The application process is online only, and the last date to submit the application form is 22 June 2026.

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC), New Delhi
Examination Name Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 (SSC CGL 2026)
Advertisement No. F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026
Post Name Various Group B (Gazetted/Non-Gazetted) and Group C posts including Assistant Section Officer, Inspector (IT/CE), Sub Inspector (CBI/NIA), Junior Statistical Officer, Assistant Audit Officer, Tax Assistant, Auditor, Accountant, Postal Assistant, and more

SSC CGL 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India, or subjects/persons of specified neighboring and migrated countries as permitted under Government of India rules, with eligibility certificate wherever required.
Essential Educational Qualification Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent for most posts, as on 01-08-2026.
Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) Bachelor's Degree in any subject with at least 60% marks in Mathematics at 12th level; or Bachelor's Degree with Statistics as one of the subjects at degree level.
Statistical Investigator Grade-II Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Demography, Population Studies, Operation Research, IT, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, or Artificial Intelligence.
Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer Bachelor's Degree is essential. Desirable qualifications include CA, CMA, CS, M.Com, MBA (Finance), or M.B.E.
Appearing Candidates Final year candidates may apply, but they must possess the essential qualification on or before 01-08-2026.
Distance Learning Degrees Accepted only if approved by Distance Education Bureau, UGC, for Central Government employment. Proof must be produced at document verification.
BRO Posts Only male candidates are eligible for vacancies reported in Border Roads Organization.

Fee must be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, and RuPay debit cards. Fee once paid will not be refunded, and applications without required fee payment will be rejected.

Correction Window Fee: Rs. 200/- for first correction and Rs. 500/- for second correction. This applies to all candidates, including fee-exempt applicants.

Also Read: SSC Language Experts Recruitment 2026 Notification Out

SSC CGL 2026 – Selection Process

Selection is through a two-tier Computer Based Examination (CBE). Marks scored in CBE conducted in multiple shifts will be normalized as per the procedure published by SSC vide Notice dated 02-06-2025. There is no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores at any stage.

Tier-I: Computer Based Examination

Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time
A. General Intelligence & Reasoning 25 50 1 hour (Sectional timer: 15 min per section)
1 hour 20 min for scribe-eligible PwBD candidates
B. General Awareness 25 50
C. Quantitative Aptitude 25 50
D. English Comprehension 25 50
Total 100 200  

Tier-I consists of Objective Type / MCQ questions, set in English and Hindi (except English Comprehension). Negative marking: 0.50 marks per wrong answer.

Tier-II: Computer Based Examination

Tier-II is conducted in Paper-I, Paper-II, and Paper-III in separate shifts/days.

Paper Section / Subject Questions Marks Duration
Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts) Section-I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning & General Intelligence 30+30 = 60 180 Session-I: 2 hrs 15 min; Section-I: 1 hr timer
Section-II: English Language & Comprehension + General Awareness 45+25 = 70 210 1 hr timer
Section-III: Computer Knowledge Test (qualifying, mandatory) 20 60 15 min
Section-IV: Data Entry Speed Test (DEST) (qualifying, mandatory) - - Session-II: 15 min
Paper-II (JSO / Statistical Investigator only) Statistics 100 200 2 hours
Paper-III (AAO / AAcO only) General Studies (Finance and Economics) 100 200 2 hours

Negative marking in Tier-II: 1 mark per wrong answer in Section-I, II, III of Paper-I; 0.50 marks in Paper-II and Paper-III. Section-III (CKT) and Section-IV (DEST) are qualifying in nature. Candidates must qualify all sections of Paper-I separately. Higher standards of CKT and DEST are set for specific posts (CSS, MEA, AFHQ, SFIO, CBIC, Postal etc.). Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available for PwBD candidates eligible for scribe.

SSC CGL 2026 – Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Paper / Section Key Topics
Tier-I: General Intelligence & Reasoning Analogies, similarities, space visualization, problem solving, arithmetic reasoning, number series, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, embedded figures, critical thinking, emotional intelligence, social intelligence
Tier-I: General Awareness Current events, India and neighbouring countries, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Policy, Scientific Research
Tier-I: Quantitative Aptitude Whole numbers, decimals, fractions, percentages, ratio, averages, SI/CI, profit & loss, discount, time & work, time & distance, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, bar charts, pie charts
Tier-I: English Comprehension Grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, writing ability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-I: Mathematical Abilities Number systems, arithmetic, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, statistics & probability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-II: English Language & Comprehension Vocabulary, grammar, synonyms, antonyms, idioms, sentence improvement, comprehension passages (current affairs + editorial based)
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-III: Computer Knowledge Computer basics, MS Office (Word/Excel/PowerPoint), Internet & e-mail, networking & cybersecurity basics
Tier-II Paper-II: Statistics Data collection, frequency distribution, central tendency, dispersion, correlation, regression, probability, sampling theory, statistical inference, ANOVA, time series, index numbers
Tier-II Paper-III: General Studies (Finance & Economics) Part A (Finance & Accounts, 80 marks): Financial accounting, GAAP, books of accounts, depreciation, bank reconciliation. Part B (Economics & Governance, 120 marks): Economic concepts, Indian economy, governance, financial markets, budget basics

SSC CGL Syllabus 2026: Tier 1 and Tier 2 Subject Wise Topics Syllbus and Exam Pattern

SSC CGL 2026 – Exam Centres

The examination will be conducted through 9 SSC Regions covering all states and union territories. Candidates must choose three examination centres, in order of priority, within the same region.

Central Region: Agra, Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Patna, Gaya Eastern Region: Kolkata, Ranchi, Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Gangtok Karnataka Kerala Region: Bengaluru, Kochi, Kozhikode, Mysuru Madhya Pradesh Region: Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur, Raipur North Eastern Region: Guwahati, Imphal, Shillong, Aizawl, Agartala Northern Region: Delhi, Jaipur, Dehradun North Western Region: Chandigarh, Shimla, Jammu, Srinagar, Amritsar Southern Region: Chennai, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Coimbatore, Madurai Western Region: Mumbai, Pune, Ahmedabad, Surat, Nagpur, Panaji

No request for change of centre will be entertained after submission of the application. SSC may add, cancel, or shift candidates to another centre depending on administrative requirements.

Competition Level Analysis for SSC CGL 2026

The overall competition level for SSC CGL 2026 is expected to remain very high because the examination attracts a massive number of graduate applicants every year across India. Even with around 12,256 vacancies, the effective competition ratio remains extremely tough for high-demand posts.

  1. Applicant volume is huge: SSC CGL is among the most sought-after central government exams, and premium posts receive intense preference from top scorers.
  2. Tier-I acts as the main screening stage: With negative marking and sectional timing, both speed and accuracy matter. Candidates targeting top posts generally need a very strong Tier-I score.
  3. Tier-II gets post-specific: Specialized posts like JSO and AAO attract a smaller but more qualified pool, making domain preparation more important than raw competition numbers.
  4. DEST and CKT can eliminate candidates: Although qualifying in nature, poor performance in these sections can disqualify otherwise strong candidates for certain posts.
  5. Physical standards create an extra filter: Certain Inspector and Sub Inspector posts require physical and medical fitness standards that are checked separately by user departments.

Who Should NOT Apply for SSC CGL 2026?

  1. Candidates who will not possess a Bachelor's Degree by 01-08-2026.
  2. Candidates who fall outside the age limit for all eligible posts even after applicable relaxation.
  3. OBC Creamy Layer candidates intending to claim OBC reservation.
  4. Candidates who cannot meet post-specific physical or medical standards for the posts they plan to prefer.
  5. Non-matriculate Ex-Servicemen or those who have not completed the required length of service.
  6. Candidates applying for JSO without the required Mathematics or Statistics qualification.
  7. Female candidates applying specifically for BRO vacancies, where only male candidates are eligible.
  8. Distance-learning degree holders without valid UGC-DEB approval for the relevant period.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying SSC CGL 2026

  1. Not generating a fresh OTR on the new SSC website.
  2. Using a pre-existing photograph instead of live photo capture where required.
  3. Uploading blurred or wrongly sized signature files.
  4. Selecting the wrong reservation category without valid supporting certificate.
  5. Ignoring post-specific standards before locking post preferences.
  6. Choosing JSO or AAO related preferences without being ready for Paper-II or Paper-III.
  7. Skipping Aadhaar-based authentication even when eligible.
  8. Waiting until the final date for application submission.
  9. Missing the answer key objection window after examination.
  10. Confusing the correction window fee structure.

How to Apply for SSC CGL 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://ssc.gov.in or through the mY SSC mobile application (available on Store). No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates who have not generated OTR on the new website must do so first. Once OTR is generated on the new website, it remains valid for all future examinations on that portal.

Candidates are advised to opt for Aadhaar-based authentication during OTR. Aadhaar-authenticated candidates get significant exemptions: their applications are not rejected for photograph/signature standards, and they need not carry photo ID at the examination venue.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Generate One-Time Registration (OTR) at https://ssc.gov.in (mandatory; use Aadhaar-based authentication if possible)
  2. Complete OTR profile and lock it
  3. Fill the Common Application Form (CAF) including live photograph capture via camera
  4. Upload scanned signature (JPEG/JPG, 10–20 KB, 6.0 cm × 2.0 cm)
  5. Fill the Examination Specific Module (post preferences, exam centre choices - 3 centres within same region in order of priority)
  6. Pay application fee of Rs. 100/- online (if applicable) by 23-06-2026
  7. Preview/Print the application to verify all details before final submission
  8. Use the correction window (29-06 to 01-07-2026) if any correction is needed (chargeable)
  9. Keep printout of application and all documents for at least 3 years

Documents to Keep Ready for Document Verification (after final selection):

  • Matriculation/Secondary School Certificate (proof of date of birth)
  • Mark sheets for all years/semesters of Graduation + Provisional/Degree Certificate
  • SC/ST Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure of the notification)
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure-X); must be valid as on closing date of application
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026–27 (issued based on income for FY 2025–26) in prescribed format (Annexure-XI)
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from competent authority specifying type of disability (as per Annexure-XII to XIII)
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate and other relevant ESM documents
  • Equivalence certificate (for equivalent qualifications other than standard degrees)
  • DEB/UGC approval letter (for Distance Learning degree holders)
  • Certificate of proficiency in State Regional/official language (for AAO/Assistant Audit Officer State Cadre)

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in | Toll Free Helpline: 1800 309 3063

Important Instructions for SSC CGL 2026 Candidates

  • Final allotment is based on merit-cum-preference, and no alternate post will be offered if a candidate fails post-specific standards after allotment.
  • OBC candidates claiming reservation must not belong to the creamy layer as on the closing date of application.
  • EWS certificate must be valid for FY 2026–27 and should be based on income for FY 2025–26.
  • PwBD candidates must choose the correct sub-category in the application form because no later change is allowed.
  • Scribe rules are strict: the scribe must complete OTR, be Aadhaar-authenticated, and cannot assist more than one candidate.
  • Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available to eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Tentative answer keys can be challenged online by paying Rs. 100 per question within the specified period only.
  • No re-evaluation or re-checking of scores is available at any stage.
  • Candidates should preserve the application form and related documents for at least 3 years.
  • Candidature remains provisional until certificates and documents are verified by the Appointing Authority.
  • Fraudulent claims of reservation status may lead to debarment from all SSC examinations.
  • Any update in exam schedule or process will be communicated through the SSC website only.
  • Applications without the required fee, unless exempted, or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Karnataka State Police Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Police Constable (Civil) posts! Published on 05-06-2026 by the Karnataka State Police, Bengaluru, this recruitment drive offers a total of 3991 vacancies - 596 posts under the Kalyana Karnataka (371(J) area) cadre and 3395 posts under the Mikkili (Okkalida) cadre - for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 18 to 33 years age group (with relaxations) possessing a PUC or equivalent qualification can apply online from 08-06-2026 to 03-07-2026. Find all details about Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026, including eligibility, vacancy details, exam pattern, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Karnataka State Police, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Bengaluru has officially released two notifications - Advertisement No. 01/Recruitment-4/2026-27 (Kalyana Karnataka / 371(J) cadre) and Advertisement No. 02/Recruitment-4/2026-27 (Mikkili/Okkalida cadre) - both dated 05.06.2026, for direct recruitment of Police Constable (Civil) posts. The notifications include complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, exam pattern, physical standards, age relaxations, reservation, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notifications before applying.

  • Download Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF – Kalyana Karnataka (596 Posts)
  • Download Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF – Mikkili/Okkalida Cadre (3395 Posts)

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Vacancy Details

A total of 3991 Police Constable (Civil) posts have been notified across two cadres. The Kalyana Karnataka cadre (Advertisement No. 01) covers 596 posts reserved for candidates belonging to the Hyderabad-Karnataka region under Article 371(J). The Mikkili (Okkalida) cadre (Advertisement No. 02) covers 3395 posts for various districts/talukas across Karnataka. The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary as per actual requirement. Category-wise and cadre-wise breakup is as follows:

Cadre 1: Kalyana Karnataka / Article 371(J) Area – 596 Posts

Post Name Pay Scale (Rs.) Direct Male Direct Female Ex-Servicemen Male Ex-Servicemen Female Third Gender Total
Police Constable (Civil) 37,500–76,100/- 398 133 46 14 5 596

Cadre 2: Mikkili (Okkalida) Cadre – 3395 Posts

Post Name Pay Scale (Rs.) Direct Male Direct Female Ex-Servicemen Male Ex-Servicemen Female Third Gender Total
Police Constable (Civil) 37,500–76,100/- 2266 755 257 82 35 3395

Note: Category-wise and district-wise breakup will be separately published. "Budakattu Community" (ಬುಡಕಟ್ಟು ಜನಾಂಗ) includes candidates belonging to Uttara Kannada, Dakshina Kannada, Mangaluru, Udupi, Chikkamagaluru, Shivamogga, Kodagu, Chamarajanagar and Mysuru forest areas - Siddi, Jenu Kuruba, Kadu Kuruba, Yerava, Soliga, Kudiya, Gowda, Hasalaru, Malekudiya and Koraga communities.

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of application. Only Indian citizens are eligible. Candidates must mandatorily possess the Kannada language proficiency as specified. Eligibility details are as follows:

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification PUC (Pre-University Certificate) or equivalent qualification (as on last date of application). Candidates appearing in PUC exam and awaiting results are NOT eligible. All subjects and marks must have been secured by the last date.
PUC Equivalents Accepted (i) Class 12 exam by CBSE or ICSE boards; (ii) Class 12 exam by other state government exam boards; (iii) Higher Secondary education course (HSC) by NIOS (National Institute of Open Schooling); (iv) Two-year BTEC course by NIOS - candidates who passed one language paper and one academic subject in distance/correspondence mode, or appeared in pre-university exam - only in one language and one subject with pass marks, are treated as equivalent to PUC
Vocational courses (GTC) As per Govt. Order No. ED 19 TVE 2019 dated 06.08.2021 - Karnataka State Vocational Training courses (GTC) are treated as equivalent to PUC only for: (i) Compassionate appointment and those already in service under the order; (ii) GTC/PUC marks considered as minimum academic qualification for competitive exams only - GTC is the minimum academic qualification
3-Year Diploma As per Govt. Order No. ED 33 TVE 2021 dated 30.09.2021 - 3-year diploma holders treated as PUC equivalent only for: (i) Direct/compassionate appointment and higher education in technical education department; (ii) Diploma passed before 2015 from a govt. technical institution must pass Kannada language test conducted by KPSC along with the department exam
Kannada Language Requirement Mandatory. Candidates who have not passed the Kannada language exam notified under Govt. Order No. GAD 43 SEN 2020 dated 5.3.2021 (as amended by Govt. Order No. GAD 102 SEN 2022 dated 24-03-2025) - i.e., those who have not passed Kannada as first or second language at PUC/equivalent level - must pass a Kannada language test with a minimum of 50 marks out of 150 (in a paper of one question booklet) to be eligible. Candidates who have previously passed the Karnataka Rajyotsava Kannada exam are exempted from this requirement.
Nationality Indian Citizens only
Prior Apprenticeship / Ex-servicemen Ex-servicemen candidates must complete their pending service period and must be serving in the present post on the last date of application; they must not be under any departmental inquiry/chargesheet at the time of applying

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Age Limit

Minimum age: 18 years. Maximum age (for direct/unreserved candidates): 33 years as on the last date of application. Age relaxation is available as per Karnataka Government norms. A one-time relaxation of 5 years in upper age limit is available for candidates who have been unjustly denied age relaxation as per Govt. Order No. GAD 262 SEN 2025 dated 29.01.2026.

Category (Direct / Male, Female & Third Gender) Maximum Age
General Merit (UR) 33 years
SC / ST / Category-1 / Cat-2A / Cat-2B / Cat-3A / Cat-3B candidates 35 years
Budakattu (Forest Tribal) candidates residing in Karnataka forest areas 38 years

 

Category (Ex-Servicemen – Male & Female) Maximum Age
General Merit (UR) 38 years
SC / ST / Category-1 / Cat-2A / Cat-2B / Cat-3A / Cat-3B candidates 40 years

Note: Ex-servicemen must have completed 5 years of service in Karnataka Police in the PC (Civil) cadre posts (P.C.R./D.A.R./K.S.R./P.I./A.R.B./K.S.I.W./Etc.) by the last date of application and must be currently serving in those posts. They must have completed the minimum service period and must not be under any departmental inquiry/chargesheet at the time of application. Ex-servicemen from Army are entitled to 3 years of additional age relaxation for the "Avara Sevaselliside Avadhi" (period of active service). "Ex-servicemen" as defined under Army Act 1950 - Gallantry Award winners are also included.

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee

Fee must be paid online only. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances.

Category Fee
General Merit and other categories (Cat-2A / Cat-2B / Cat-3A / Cat-3B) Rs. 750/-
SC / ST / Category-1 / Ex-Servicemen / Third Gender candidates Rs. 500/-

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process

Selection will be through the following stages. Candidates must qualify each stage to proceed to the next. No marks are awarded for Physical Standard Test and Endurance Test - these are qualifying in nature only.

  • Stage 1 – Written Examination (Offline-OMR type): Objective type written test of 100 questions / 100 marks / 1½ hours (90 minutes). Conducted at district exam centres across the state. Candidates must mandatorily download the admit card from the authority website and carry one original identity proof to the exam centre.
  • Stage 2 – Physical Standard Test (PST) & Endurance Test (ET): Candidates who qualify the written exam at a ratio of 1:5 (per vacancy) will be called for PST and ET. Dates will be published on https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/ or https://ksp-recruitment.in/
  • Stage 3 – Medical Examination: Candidates who qualify PST and ET will be called for medical examination. Only medically fit candidates will be eligible for final appointment.
  • Stage 4 – Final Selection: Candidates who qualify all above stages will be shortlisted at a ratio of 1:2 per vacancy based on written exam marks. Final appointment is made based on merit after medical examination, as per Govt. Order dated 13.04.2026.

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 – Exam Pattern & Syllabus

Written Examination Pattern (Offline-OMR Type):

Paper Subject Total Questions Total Marks Duration
Written Exam General Knowledge, Science, Geography, History, Indian Constitution, National Integration, Current Affairs, Mental Aptitude, and General Studies 100 100 1½ Hours (90 Minutes)

Written Exam Schedule:

Cadre Date Time
Kalyana Karnataka (371(J) – 596 Posts) 02-08-2026 2:30 PM to 4:00 PM
Mikkili / Okkalida Cadre – 3395 Posts 02-08-2026 10:30 AM to 12:00 PM
Mandatory Kannada Language Test To be notified after Written Exam result (applicable for those who have not passed Kannada at 10th/PUC level)

Candidates must score a minimum of 30 marks in the written examination as per recruitment (amendment) rules 2020 to be eligible as a competitive candidate.

Negative Marking: Each question has 4 options; only one correct answer must be marked. For every wrong answer, 0.25 marks (1/4th) will be deducted. In OMR-based answers, if 5 or more options are filled or the answer/option is struck out after the final bell and within 5 minutes, 0.25 marks will be deducted for each such question.

Physical Standard Test (PST) Standards:

Category Minimum Height Chest (For Male) Minimum Weight (For Female)
Male (General) & Ex-Servicemen (Male) 168 cm 86 cm (unexpanded); minimum expansion 5 cm -
Female & Third Gender candidates 157 cm - 45 kg
Budakattu (Forest Tribal) Male candidates from Karnataka forest areas 155 cm Chest expansion of minimum 5 cm from fully expanded; overall chest not less than 75 cm -
Budakattu (Forest Tribal) Female candidates from Karnataka forest areas 150 cm - -

Endurance Test (ET) Standards:

Category Event Qualifying Standard / Distance
Male & Ex-Servicemen (Male) candidates 1600 Metre Run Within 6 minutes 30 seconds
High Jump or Long Jump (3 chances only) High Jump: Not less than 1.20 m; Long Jump: Not less than 3.80 m
Shot Put (7.26 kg) – 3 chances only Not less than 5.60 metres
   
Female, Third Gender & Ex-Servicemen (Female) candidates 400 Metre Run Within 2 minutes
High Jump or Long Jump (3 chances only) High Jump: Not less than 0.90 m; Long Jump: Not less than 2.50 m
Shot Put (4 kg) – 3 chances only Not less than 3.75 metres
   

Note: Candidates must qualify ALL events in the Endurance Test. Candidates who fail any event are disqualified from the ET. No marks are awarded for the ET; it is qualifying in nature only. Candidates are advised to practise at least one month before the ET date.

Medical Examination Standards (key disqualification criteria):

  • Vision: Distant vision: 6/9 or 6/9 (best eye) and 6/12 (other eye) without glasses; Near vision: 0/6 and 0/8. Each eye must have full field of vision. Any condition such as squint, colour blindness, or any pathological condition of the eye is disqualifying.
  • Hearing: Rinne's Test, Weber's Test, and Tests for Vertigo - any deficiency is disqualifying.
  • Varicose Veins with flatfoot: Disqualifying
  • Speech impediment: Disqualifying
  • Musculoskeletal Deformities (disqualifying): Knock Knees, Flat Foot, Bow Legs, Cubitus Valgus, Cubitus Varus, or any other defect observed during examination
  • Any physical or mental deficiency that may hinder the performance of police duties is disqualifying

How to Apply for Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online only through the Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) portal. No other mode of application will be accepted. Only one application per post - candidates applying for both cadres must submit separate applications for each. Candidates must read all instructions before applying.

Apply Online at: https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/

Documents / Details Required at the time of Application:

  • Recent passport-size photograph in JPEG format (50 KB to 200 KB)
  • Signature in black ink on white paper in JPEG format (50 KB to 70 KB)
  • Valid mobile number and e-mail ID (must remain active until recruitment process is complete)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Physical Test / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Aadhaar Card (mandatory)
  • Marks cards and pass certificates of all subjects/semesters of the qualified post (as on last date of application)
  • Date of birth proof: SSLC / 10th standard marks card or relevant document
  • Discharge certificate and pension book / pension payment order (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • Caste / Reservation certificates in prescribed Government of India formats (SC/ST: Form 'D'; Cat-1: Form 'E'; Cat-2A/2B/3A/3B: Form 'F') - must be currently valid and issued by Tahsildar or competent authority with seal, signature, and bar code/hologram
  • Rural reservation certificate (Form 1 or Form 2 as applicable) - signed by school Headmaster and Field Education Officer
  • Kannada Medium Study certificate (for KMS reservation), if applicable
  • Project Displaced Person (PDP) certificate, if applicable
  • Income and caste certificate from Tahsildar in prescribed format (for OBC categories)
  • NOC / Service certificate from employer (for candidates currently in service)
  • One original photo identity proof: Passport / Driving License / PAN Card / Service ID Card issued by State/Central Govt., PSU or Private Companies / Bank Passbook with Photo / Registered Deeds with Photo / Caste Certificate with Photo / Ex-servicemen's Pension Book / NREGS Job Card / Voter ID (EPIC) / Aadhaar Card

Training after Recruitment: Candidates appointed through direct recruitment must undergo 9 months of basic foundational training at Police Training Schools (DIGI/IGPI). Candidates who fail to qualify in the prescribed examinations during training will be dismissed from service.

Probation Period: Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years and 6 months after appointment through direct recruitment.

For queries: Helpline – 080-23460460 (10:00 AM to 6:00 PM). Queries regarding written exam and marks only will be entertained by the Authority.

Important Instructions for Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates applying online must verify all information carefully before final submission; take a printout of the application and retain the fee challan until completion of the recruitment process. No changes/modifications to the submitted application will be permitted thereafter.
  • Only Online applications are accepted; no hard copies are to be sent to the authority.
  • Candidates applying for both cadres must submit a separate application for each; apply only for one post per application.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the Creamy Layer are NOT entitled to reservation; they must apply under General Merit.
  • Caste/reservation certificates must be currently valid (not expired) as on the last date of application; certificates for SC/ST/Cat-1 may be of any validity period; for OBC categories (Cat-2A/2B/3A/3B), the certificate must be valid for 5 years. Only caste certificates obtained for employment purposes will be considered (not certificates obtained for educational or other benefits).
  • Married female candidates from OBC/other categories must produce caste certificate issued in their husband's name (not father's name) to claim reservation.
  • The Authority will not conduct any correspondence with candidates. Candidates are responsible for regularly checking the official website for updates, notifications, and corrigendum.
  • Mobile number and email provided in the application are final; no changes will be entertained subsequently.
  • Fee payment last date: If the last date falls on a public holiday, the next working day shall be the last date.
  • Candidates currently in service / having served must obtain NOC/permission from employer and submit it to the recruitment authority; service details must be mentioned in the application.
  • Any misconduct or malpractice at any stage will result in immediate disqualification from all stages of the selection process.
  • Written examination is conducted by Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA); evaluated answer sheets will be shared with Karnataka Police and the Recruitment Board for further process.
  • Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Bengaluru only.
  • The authority reserves the right to cancel or modify the recruitment process at any stage without prior notice.

Check complete details for SSC SSA/UDC LDCE 2025 Notification. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

⚡ Key Highlights
