📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Notice for Senior Secretariat Assistant / Upper Division Clerk Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, 2025 on 05 June 2026 vide No. 4/1/2026-CONF-5. The examination will be held tentatively in July-August 2026 in Delhi NCR only for making additions to the Select List of Senior Secretariat Assistant/Upper Division Clerk Grade [Pay Level-4, Group 'C' Non-Gazetted (Rs. 25,500 - Rs. 81,100)] across 10 participating Services/Cadres. This is a Limited Departmental Competitive Examination open only to eligible serving government employees (JSA/LDC) of the specified participating cadres. The application process is active from 05 June 2026 to 26 June 2026, and candidates can access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission, Block No.12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Department of Personnel and Training, Government of India, has officially released the Notice for Senior Secretariat Assistant/Upper Division Clerk Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, 2025. This is a departmental (internal) exam restricted to eligible Junior Secretariat Assistants (JSA) / Lower Division Clerks (LDC) of the 10 specified participating services/cadres. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full advertisement before applying online.

  • Download SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 Official Notification PDF

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 Overview

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Block No.12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003
Notice No. No. 4/1/2026-CONF-5
Exam Name Senior Secretariat Assistant / Upper Division Clerk Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, 2025
Post Name Senior Secretariat Assistant (SSA) / Upper Division Clerk (UDC) Grade
Post Group Group 'C' Non-Gazetted
Pay Scale Pay Level-4 (Rs. 25,500 - Rs. 81,100) as per 7th CPC
Total Vacancies 227 Posts
Type of Examination Limited Departmental Competitive Examination (internal - for serving JSA/LDC only)
Application Mode Online only (via SSC official website ssc.gov.in)
Selection Mode Part-I: Computer Based Examination (Paper-I, 200 marks) + Written Examination (Paper-II, 100 marks) + Part-II: Evaluation of Service Records / APARs (100 marks)
Examination Centre Delhi NCR only
Official Website https://ssc.gov.in / https://sscnr.nic.in

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 Important Dates

Event Date & Time
Start Date for Online Applications 05 June 2026
Last Date for Online Applications 26 June 2026 (2300 hours)
Last Date for Receipt of Printed Application (through proper channel) - General 03 July 2026 (1800 hours)
Last Date for Receipt of Printed Application - Candidates abroad/A&N Islands/Lakshadweep 10 July 2026 (1800 hours)
Schedule of Computer Based Examination (Tentative) July - August 2026
Written Examination (Paper-II) To be notified later
Evaluation of Service Records (APARs) To be notified later

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 - Vacancy Details

A total of 227 tentative vacancies have been announced across 10 participating Services/Cadres for the year 2025. The vacancies are distributed across UR, SC, and ST categories. PwBD reservations are applicable as per each cadre's reporting. The number of vacancies is subject to availability as reported by the respective Service/Cadre Controlling Authorities (CCAs) and may change.

Sl. No. Name of Service / Cadre / Organization UR SC ST Total PwBD
1 Central Passport Organisation, Ministry of External Affairs 46 19 11 76 04
2 Armed Forces Headquarters Clerical Service 31 07 04 42 03 (VH, HH & OH)
3 Railway Board Secretariat Clerical Service, Ministry of Railways 13 04 02 19 03 PH (2B/LV, 01 D/HH) 
4 Central Administrative Tribunal 15 06 03 24 02 (01-OH, 01-VH)
5 Central Vigilance Commission 05 01 - 06 01 (any category)
6 Election Commission of India 09 02 01 12 01 (UR-OH)
7 Forest Survey of India, Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change 03 01 - 04 -
8 National Technical Research Organisation 07 01 - 08 -
9 Directorate General, Defence Estates 07 01 - 08 -
10 Office of Development Commissioner, Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises 22 04 02 28 01 (VH)
Total 158 46 23 227  

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 - Eligibility Criteria

This is a Limited Departmental Competitive Examination open only to serving government employees (JSA/LDC) of the specified participating cadres. Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the crucial date for their respective cadre. The eligibility conditions prescribed in the Notice shall not be relaxed under any circumstances.

Eligibility Conditions (Crucial Date and Service Requirements - Post-wise)

Name of Service / Cadre Crucial Date Conditions of Eligibility
Central Passport Organisation, Ministry of External Affairs 01.07.2025 Junior Passport Assistant with 6 years' service in the grade rendered after appointment thereto on regular basis and possessing a Bachelor's degree of a recognized university or its equivalent.
Armed Forces Headquarters Clerical Service 01.07.2025 Must have rendered not less than five years approved continuous service in the JSA Grade on the crucial date. Provided that if appointed on the result of a Competitive Examination (including LDCE), the results should have been announced not less than five years before the crucial date and he/she should have rendered not less than four years' approved continuous service. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.
Railway Board Secretariat Clerical Service, Ministry of Railways 01.08.2025 (A) Length of Service: Not less than five years' approved and continuous service in the Lower Division Grade on the crucial date. If appointed on the result of a Competitive Examination (including LDCE), results should have been announced not less than five years before the crucial date and at least four years' approved and continuous service rendered. (B) Typewriting Test: Unless exempted, should have passed the typewriting test on or before the date of notification. (C) Age: Not more than 50 years on the crucial date (relaxable as notified by Central Government).
Central Administrative Tribunal 01.01.2025 Five years regular service in the Lower Division Clerk Grade. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.
Central Vigilance Commission 01.01.2025 Junior Secretariat Assistant who has rendered five years of regular service in the level in pay matrix.
Election Commission of India 01.07.2025 Junior Secretariat Assistant/Lower Division Clerk who has completed a minimum of three years regular service in the grade. If appointed on the result of a competitive examination (including LDCE), results should have been announced not less than three years before the crucial date and at least 2 years' approved and continuous service rendered. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.
Forest Survey of India, Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change 01.01.2025 Lower Division Clerk having five years of regular service in the grade in Forest Survey of India. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.
National Technical Research Organisation 01.01.2025 Junior Secretariat Assistant/Lower Division Clerk of NTRO having five years of regular service in the grade. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.
Directorate General, Defence Estates 01.01.2025 Hindi Typist who has rendered five years regular service. Age: Not more than 45 years of age on the crucial date.
Office of Development Commissioner, Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises 01.01.2025 Lower Division Clerk of MSME-DO in the Level-2 as per 7th CPC with five years of regular service in the Grade. JSAs on deputation to ex-cadre posts with approval and those having lien in JSA grade are also eligible.

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 - Age Limit

Some Cadre Controlling Authorities have prescribed age criteria for appearing in this examination (refer eligibility table above). Candidate should not be more than the age prescribed by their respective cadre/Cadre Controlling Authority as on the crucial date for their cadre. The following age relaxations are permissible beyond the upper age limit:

Code Category Age Relaxation permissible beyond upper age limit
01 SC / ST 05 Years
08 Defence Personnel disabled in operation during hostilities with any foreign country or in a disturbed area and released as a consequence thereof 03 Years
09 Defence Personnel disabled in operation during hostilities with any foreign country or in a disturbed area and released as a consequence thereof (SC/ST) 08 Years

For the purposes of claiming age concessions, a certificate from the Director General, Resettlement, Ministry of Defence will be accepted as documentary evidence in support of the claim of Disabled Defence Service Personnel.

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 - Application Fee

The notification does not mention any application fee for this Limited Departmental Competitive Examination. This is an internal departmental exam restricted to serving government employees of the specified cadres. Candidates are advised to check the official notification on ssc.gov.in for the latest information on fee.

Category Examination Fee
All Eligible Departmental Candidates No fee mentioned (Limited Departmental Competitive Examination - internal)

SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025 - Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The examination consists of two parts: Part-I (Computer Based & Written Examination, 300 marks) and Part-II (Evaluation of Record of Service/APARs, 100 marks). Total marks = 400. Final selection will be made on the basis of aggregate performance in all parts. This is a competitive and not a qualifying examination.

Part-I: Computer Based & Written Examination (300 Marks)

Subject Paper No. No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time Duration Mode
General Awareness Paper-I 100 200 2 hours (2 hours 40 minutes for candidates eligible for Scribe) Computer Based Examination (Objective Type Multiple Choice)
English Language and Comprehension 100
Noting, Drafting & Office Procedure Paper-II -- 100 2 hours (2 hours 40 minutes for candidates eligible for Scribe) Written Examination (Descriptive / Conventional type)

Note: There will be negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in Computer Based Examination (Paper-I). Candidates have to attempt the examination in the language (English/Hindi) as opted in the online Application Form. There will be no Question Paper in Braille for Visually Handicapped candidates.

Part-II: Evaluation of Record of Service (100 Marks)

Marks 100 marks earmarked for evaluation of record of service (APARs - Annual Performance Assessment Reports)
Usage Marks obtained in Part-II will be added for ascertaining the rank of the candidate. Once the APARs have been evaluated, no request for change in APAR grading will be entertained by the Commission under any circumstances.

Syllabus of the Examination

Paper Subject Syllabus / Topics
Paper-I (CBE) General Awareness Questions aimed at testing General Awareness of the environment around and its application to society. Current events; matters of everyday observations and experience in their scientific aspect; History, Culture, Geography, Economics, Science, General Politics and Scientific research relating to India and neighboring countries.
Paper-I (CBE) English Language and Comprehension Understanding and knowledge of English language: Vocabulary, spelling, grammar, sentence structure, synonyms, antonyms, sentence completion, phrases and idiomatic use of words, comprehension of a passage. Questions will be of matriculation level.
Paper-II (Written/Descriptive) Noting, Drafting & Office Procedure Knowledge of office procedure in the Secretariat and Attached Offices; ability to write and understand notes and drafts. Candidates are required to study the Manual of Office Procedure (including Ministry of Railways version), the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha, Hand Book of orders issued by Ministry of Home Affairs regarding use of Hindi for official purpose of the Union, and Reservation Policy for SC/STs in services and orders issued by Government thereon from time to time.

Mode of Selection

Step 1 Based on performance in Paper-I (CBE) of Part-I, candidates will be shortlisted for evaluation of Paper-II (Descriptive Paper) of Part-I. Candidates not qualifying Paper-I will not be evaluated for Paper-II and will not be considered for further selection.
Step 2 Based on aggregate performance in Paper-I and Paper-II of Part-I, candidates will be shortlisted for evaluation of record of service (Part-II). Candidates not qualifying Part-I aggregate will not be eligible for Part-II evaluation.
Step 3 Final selection will be made on the basis of aggregate performance of candidates in Paper-I (CBE) + Paper-II (Descriptive) of Part-I and Part-II (APAR Evaluation). The Commission may fix minimum qualifying marks individually for Paper-I and Paper-II of Part-I.

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Marks obtained in Paper-II (Descriptive)
2nd Tie-Break Marks obtained in part (b) of Paper-I (English Language and Comprehension)
3rd Tie-Break Date of birth - older candidates placed higher
4th Tie-Break Alphabetical order in which the names of the candidates appear

How to Apply for SSC SSA/UDC Limited Departmental Competitive Examination 2025

The Application Form must be submitted only in online mode on the SSC website https://ssc.gov.in. The process consists of two parts: (i) One Time Registration (OTR) and (ii) Filling of online Application Form for the Examination. Candidates who have not generated their OTR on the new website (https://ssc.gov.in) will be required to do so; the earlier OTR generated on the old website (https://ssc.nic.in) will not be functional for the new website.

After completion of online Application Form, the printed copy accompanied with necessary documents must be duly forwarded by the respective Service/Cadre Controlling Authority to "The Regional Director, Staff Selection Commission (Northern Region), Block No.12, C.G.O. Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi-110003" so as to reach by 03.07.2026 (1800 hrs). Only those candidates whose application is forwarded by their Head of Department/Head of Office will be considered for admission to this Examination.

Documents to be Uploaded with Online Application:

  • Certified true copy of the first page of the Service Book duly attested by Head of Department/Head of Office (showing Name, Father's Name, Husband's Name, Nationality, SC/ST Name if applicable, Date of Birth)
  • Certified true copy of particulars of service attested by Head of Department/Head of Office (posts held, scale of pay, nature of appointment - substantive/officiating/permanent/temporary)
  • Copy of certificate of essential educational qualification, if applicable
  • Form of certificate for disabled Defence Personnel while in service, wherever applicable
  • Certificate of disability in case of PwBD candidates
  • Live photograph captured through the application module (plain background, no cap/mask/glasses, full frontal view)
  • Scanned signature in JPEG format (10 to 20 KB; image dimension about 6.0 cm width x 2.0 cm height)

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in

Important Instructions for SSC SSA/UDC Departmental Examination 2025 Candidates

  • This is a Limited Departmental Competitive Examination - only eligible serving JSA/LDC employees of the specified 10 participating cadres can apply.
  • Only one application form should be submitted; submission of multiple applications may lead to rejection of candidature.
  • After completion of online application, the printed copy must be forwarded by the Head of Department/Office through proper channel to reach SSC (NR) by 03.07.2026 (1800 hrs); applications not forwarded through proper channel will not be considered.
  • A candidate who resigns, quits service, or whose services are terminated after applying will not be eligible for appointment on the result of this examination.
  • No change in entries made in the online application form shall be allowed at any stage under any circumstances.
  • The examination shall be conducted in Delhi NCR only; no centre will be at any Indian mission abroad. Candidates serving abroad will have to appear in India at their own expense.
  • Admission Certificate for CBE will be issued online by uploading on the website of the Northern Regional Office; it will not be issued by post. Candidates are advised to regularly visit https://sscnr.nic.in.
  • Candidates must carry their Departmental Identity Card (Original) along with hard copy of Admission Certificate for appearing in the examination.
  • Candidates who have not undergone Aadhaar Based Authentication must carry at least two passport size recent colour photographs and Original valid Photo-ID proof with Date of Birth at the examination centre.
  • There is no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores of any stage/paper(s) of the Examination; no correspondence in this regard shall be entertained.
  • Tentative Answer Keys of CBE will be placed on the SSC website after the examination; candidates can submit online representations within the time limit on payment of Rs. 50/- per question (non-refundable).
  • Candidates are advised to opt for Aadhaar-Based Authentication during the OTR process.
  • The appearance of a candidate at the examination must match the photograph in the application form - no change in appearance is permitted.
  • All communications should be addressed to "The Regional Director, Staff Selection Commission (Northern Region), Block No. 12, C.G.O. Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi-110003" with subject line "Senior Secretariat Assistant/Upper Division Clerk Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, 2025".
  • For Helpdesk: SSC-HQ toll-free 1800 309 3063; SSC (NR) New Delhi: 011-24363343.
  • Legal disputes, if any, shall be subject to courts/tribunals having jurisdiction over the place of the Regional Office of SSC where the candidate appeared in the examination.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the notification for the Junior Secretariat Assistant (JSA) / Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination (LDCE), 2025 vide Notice No. 4/4/2026-CONF-5, to fill 149 vacancies across 12 Central Government departments and cadres. The online application window is open from 05th June 2026 to 26th June 2026 (up to 11:00 PM). Please note this is a limited departmental examination - only existing regular Group 'C' / Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS) employees of the participating cadres are eligible, and every application must be forwarded through proper channel by the Head of Department/Office. The Computer Based Examination is tentatively scheduled for July–August 2026 and will be held in Delhi-NCR only. Check the complete eligibility, vacancy break-up, exam pattern, and step-by-step process below.

FOLLOW US

Important: Who Can Apply (and Who Cannot)

Because this is an LDCE (Limited Departmental Competitive Examination), eligibility is far narrower than an open recruitment. Read this before anything else - it is the most common point of confusion.

You CAN apply only if all of the following are true:

  • You are a regularly appointed Group 'C' employee or Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS) in Level-1 / Grade Pay ₹1,800 of one of the 12 participating cadres listed below.
  • You have completed at least 3 years of regular service in that grade as on your cadre's crucial date.
  • You have passed Class 12th (Higher Secondary) or equivalent from a recognised Board.
  • Your application is forwarded by your Head of Department / Head of Office to SSC (Northern Region).

You CANNOT apply if:

  • You are a fresh candidate / general public with no qualifying government service - this exam is not open to private-sector or unemployed applicants.
  • You work in a department that is not among the 12 participating cadres.
  • Your application is not forwarded through proper channel - direct/self-submitted hard copies will be summarily rejected.

The 149 posts are spread across 12 services/cadres, as reported by their respective Cadre Controlling Authorities. The reservation break-up is given below:

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 Notification Out

The official notice for the Junior Secretariat Assistant / Lower Division Clerk Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, 2025 has been uploaded on the SSC website on 05.06.2026. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification PDF - especially the eligibility conditions and crucial date applicable to their own cadre - before applying.

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 Notification PDF - Download from ssc.gov.in

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Important Dates

Apply well before the deadline. SSC will not be responsible for failure to submit on account of heavy website load in the closing days, and no application received after the last date and time will be entertained under any circumstances.

Event Date
Notification Released 05-06-2026
Online Application Begins 05-06-2026
Last Date & Time to Apply Online 26-06-2026 (11:00 PM)
Forwarded Hard Copy to Reach SSC-NR 03-07-2026 (6:00 PM)
Hard Copy Deadline (Abroad / A&N / Lakshadweep) 10-07-2026 (6:00 PM)
Computer Based Examination July – August 2026 (Tentative)
Admission Certificate Download 3–4 days before the exam

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification

Candidates must have passed the 12th standard (Higher Secondary) examination from a recognised Board or equivalent. For the Railway Board Secretariat Clerical Service, candidates are additionally required to pass a typing test (35 w.p.m. in English or 30 w.p.m. in Hindi on computer) within one year of appointment.

Length of Service

A minimum of three years of regular service as a Group 'C' employee / MTS in Level-1 (Grade Pay ₹1,800) in the respective cadre, counted as on the crucial date applicable to that cadre.

Crucial Date (Varies by Cadre)

The crucial date for counting approved service and age is not the same for every department. Check yours carefully:

Crucial Date Applicable Cadres
01-07-2025 Central Passport Organisation, MEA (Cadre Cell), Bureau of Police Research & Development
01-08-2025 Railway Board Secretariat Clerical Service
01-01-2025 Central Administrative Tribunal, O/o Registrar General of India, NTRO, Dept. of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, O/o Development Commissioner (MSME), Central Translation Bureau, Official Language Wing

Age Limit & Relaxation

For most participating cadres, the maximum age is 45 years (50 years for SC/ST) as on the crucial date. Candidates should confirm the exact age condition for their own cadre from the notification. Permissible relaxation beyond the upper age limit:

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years
Defence personnel disabled in operations 3 years
Defence personnel disabled in operations (SC/ST) 8 years

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Application Fee

For this Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, the notice does not prescribe an application fee for submitting the form. (A separate, non-refundable charge of ₹50 per question applies only if a candidate later chooses to challenge the tentative answer key - this is unrelated to the application itself.)

How to Apply for SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025

The process has two parts - One Time Registration (OTR) followed by the application - plus a mandatory offline forwarding step. Follow these steps:

  1. Generate OTR on the new website. Visit ssc.gov.in and click "Register Now." Any OTR created on the old site (ssc.nic.in) will not work on the new portal.
  2. Opt for Aadhaar-Based Authentication during OTR (recommended). Candidates who skip Aadhaar must upload the prescribed proof documents.
  3. Log in with your Registration Number and password, then open the JSA/LDC LDCE 2025 link under the "Live Exam" tab and click Apply.
  4. Fill the application - service details, cadre, crucial date, length of service, and age-relaxation code (if any). Information auto-filled from OTR is non-editable; use "Modify Registration" if a correction is needed.
  5. Capture your live photograph and upload your signature as per the specifications (see below).
  6. Upload the required documents (listed below) and Submit. Take a printout of the submitted application.
  7. Forward the hard copy through proper channel. Your Head of Department / Head of Office must forward the printed application along with service documents to: The Regional Director, Staff Selection Commission (Northern Region), Block No. 12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, so as to reach by 03-07-2026 (6:00 PM) - or 10-07-2026 for candidates abroad/A&N/Lakshadweep.

Critical: Only applications forwarded by the Head of Department/Office will be admitted. A correctly submitted online form alone is not enough.

Documents to Keep Ready Before You Start

  • Certified true copy of the first page of your Service Book (attested by HoD/HoO)
  • Certified particulars of service (posts held, scale of pay, nature of appointment)
  • Class 12th certificate / educational qualification proof (if applicable)
  • Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST), if claiming reservation
  • Disability certificate (Annexure-VII / VIII) for PwBD candidates
  • A recent passport-size photo for the live capture reference, and a scanned signature

Photograph & Signature Specifications

  • Photograph: Captured live via webcam/phone, in a well-lit area with a plain background. No cap, mask, or spectacles. Face must fill the on-screen red rectangle. Pre-existing/scanned photos are rejected.
  • Signature: JPEG/JPG, 10–20 KB, dimensions about 6.0 cm × 2.0 cm. Sign so the signature fills at least 80% of the box. Blurred or miniature signatures are rejected.

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Exam Pattern & Scheme

The examination carries 260 marks in total - 200 for Part-I (the exams) and 60 for Part-II (service record). Note that Paper-I is descriptive (written) and Paper-II is the objective Computer Based Test (CBT).

Part-I: Written + Computer Based Examination (200 Marks)

Paper Type Section Questions Max Marks Duration
Paper-I Descriptive (Written) Short Essay – 100 1 hr 30 min*
Paper-II Objective (CBT) (a) Language - General English or Samanya Hindi 50 50 2 hrs*
Paper-II Objective (CBT) (b) General Knowledge 50 50 (combined)

*Scribe-eligible candidates get 2 hours for Paper-I and 2 hours 40 minutes for Paper-II, plus 20 minutes compensatory time per hour.

  • Negative marking of 0.25 marks per wrong answer applies to Paper-II (CBT) only. There is no negative marking in the descriptive Paper-I.
  • Candidates must attempt the exam in the language (English/Hindi) chosen in the application form.

Part-II: Evaluation of Record of Service (60 Marks)

60 marks are reserved for evaluation of the APARs (Annual Performance Appraisal Reports). These marks are added to determine the final rank. Once evaluated, no request to change APAR grading is entertained.

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Selection Process

This is a competitive, not qualifying examination. Selection proceeds stage by stage:

  1. Paper-II (CBT) qualifying: Candidates must clear the minimum marks fixed for Paper-II to have their Paper-I evaluated.
  2. Paper-I (Descriptive) evaluation: Only those qualifying in Paper-II have their descriptive paper assessed.
  3. APAR evaluation (Part-II): Based on the aggregate of Paper-I + Paper-II, shortlisted candidates move to service-record evaluation.
  4. Final merit: Prepared on the combined total of Paper-I + Paper-II + Part-II (APAR) = 260 marks, cadre-wise, subject to reservation. Ties are resolved by Paper-I marks, then Paper-II part (a), then Paper-II total, then date of birth (older ranked higher), then alphabetical order.

SSC JSA / LDC LDCE 2025 - Syllabus

  • Paper-I (Short Essay): One essay of 400–500 words on any of the specified subjects.
  • Paper-II (a) Language: Simple composition and applied grammar (in the chosen English/Hindi medium).
  • Paper-II (b) General Knowledge: Current events, matters of everyday observation and experience, and general information about India.

SSC JSA / LDC Salary 2026

Selected candidates are appointed in Pay Level-2 (₹19,900 – ₹63,200 per month) under the 7th CPC, as Group 'C' Non-Gazetted staff. In addition to basic pay, employees are entitled to Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance, and other benefits as admissible under Central Government rules for the respective department.

Common Reasons SSC Applications Get Rejected

A large share of LDCE applications fail on avoidable mistakes. Double-check all of these before submitting:

  • Application not forwarded by the Head of Department/Office (the single biggest cause of rejection).
  • Multiple applications submitted for the same exam.
  • Photograph with a cap/spectacles, dark background, blurred, or a pre-existing (scanned) photo instead of a live capture.
  • Signature that is blurred, miniature, or below the size norm.
  • Wrong OTR details - name, father's/mother's name, or date of birth not matching the matriculation certificate (OTR data is non-editable).
  • Hard copy reaching SSC-NR after the deadline, or incomplete documents.

What to Do If the SSC Website Is Slow

Traffic on the SSC portal spikes near the closing date. If you face issues:

  • Apply during off-peak hours - late at night or early morning - rather than on the last day.
  • Clear your browser cache/cookies, or use an incognito/private tab.
  • Try a different browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge) or switch between Wi-Fi and mobile data.
  • Keep all documents pre-scanned and ready in the correct format so a session timeout doesn't cost you the whole form.
  • Save a draft frequently and take a printout immediately after final submission.

Exam Day Guidelines

  • The exam is conducted in Delhi-NCR only; there is no centre abroad. A candidate posted abroad must appear in India at their own expense.
  • The Admission Certificate is issued online via sscnr.nic.in (3–4 days before the exam); it is never sent by post.
  • Carry your original Departmental Identity Card along with a hard copy of the Admission Certificate.
  • Candidates not Aadhaar-authenticated during OTR must report early with two passport-size photographs and an original photo ID showing date of birth.
  • No Travelling Allowance is paid by SSC.
  • Mobile phones, Bluetooth/wireless devices, smartwatches, and electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited. SSC conducts post-exam log, biometric, and CCTV analysis, and malpractice attracts debarment of 1 to 7 years depending on the offence.

Helpline & Official Links

  • Help Desk (SSC-HQ): 1800-309-3063 (Toll-free)
  • SSC (Northern Region): 011-24363343
  • Official Websites: ssc.gov.in (HQ) · sscnr.nic.in (Northern Region)

Disclaimer: The above information is compiled from SSC Notice No. 4/4/2026-CONF-5 dated 05.06.2026. Candidates must verify all details - especially the eligibility and crucial date applicable to their own cadre - from the official notification on ssc.gov.in before applying.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Deputy Manager (E2), Manager (E3), and Senior Manager (E4) posts! Published on 16-05-2026 at Hindustan Copper Limited Official Website, this new recruitment drive offers 70 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the minimum 18 years and above age group and possessing a relevant Bachelor’s Degree / Post Graduate Degree / CA / CMA / MBA / MBBS qualification as per post requirement can apply online from 05-06-2026 to 25-06-2026. Find all details about the HCL Executive new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Hindustan Copper Limited Executive Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL) has officially released Advertisement No. Estt./1/2033/2025-26 dated 16/05/2026 for recruitment to 70 posts of Executives in Grade E2, E3, and E4 across various disciplines. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, PwBD sub-category classification, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official HCL website.

HCL Executive Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of reckoning (01-05-2026). Only Indian Nationals aged 18 years and above are eligible to apply. HCL Executive Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application processing fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the Payment Gateway / NEFT Online Transfer via HCL's website only. No other form of remittance shall be accepted. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC / EWS Candidates Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred only) + applicable Bank charges
SC / ST / PwBD / Internal (HCL) Candidates Exempted (No fee)

HCL Executive Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process consists of a single stage - Personal Interview (PI) carrying 100% weightage. Candidates will be shortlisted for PI based on experience and additional qualifications if applications exceed the zone of consideration. The final selection and merit list will be based solely on performance in the Personal Interview.

Stage 1 - Shortlisting Criteria (if applications exceed zone of consideration)

Criteria Marks
For every completed year of relevant experience beyond minimum requirement 1 Mark per year
For every additional higher qualification over the minimum essential qualification (relevant cadre/discipline only) 1 Mark each, subject to maximum 2 Marks
Tie-break: Higher company turnover gets higher rank –

Stage 2 - Personal Interview: 100% Weightage

Zone of Consideration (Shortlisting Ratio for Interview)

Number of Vacancies (Category-wise) Number of Candidates to be Called for Personal Interview
Single Vacancy 10 candidates
More than 1 vacancy 5 candidates per vacancy (1:5 ratio)

How to Apply for HCL Executive Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the HCL website career section (www.hindustancopper.com). No manual/paper applications will be entertained.

The online portal opens on 05-06-2026 (11:00 AM) and closes on 25-06-2026 (Till Midnight).

Candidates must register on the HCL website under the career section before filling the application. Candidates are advised to read the "How to fill the Online Application form" instructions on the website before applying.

Documents to be Submitted / Verified at the time of Personal Interview:

  • Date of Birth proof – Xth Standard Passing Certificate or School Leaving Certificate / Birth Certificate
  • XIIth Standard Passing Certificate
  • Mark Sheets and Degree/Diploma Certificates of Graduate and/or Post-Graduate and/or Professional Qualification as applicable
  • Experience Certificates for each organization (with designation, department, date of joining/leaving, job description)
  • If working in PSU/Govt.: documentary evidence of working in next below grade and scale of pay
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer if working in PSU / Government organization
  • Salary Slips (last 3 months), Bank Statement, and latest Form-16 (for private/bank employees)
  • Caste/Category Certificate – SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD as applicable, in prescribed Government of India format
  • Original Photo ID proof (Aadhar / Driving License / Voter-ID)
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded) duly issued by University/Institute

Documents to be produced at the venue of Personal Interview as per call letter. Non-production of original documents will debar candidates from appearing in the interview.

Apply Online at: https://www.hindustancopper.com/RecruitmentDraft/CandidateLogin/141

Important Instructions for HCL Executive Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Nationals of 18 years and above are eligible to apply.
  • Date of reckoning for age, experience, and qualification is 01-05-2026.
  • Experience acquired in Training / Consultancy / Teaching / Fellowship / Internship / Apprenticeship / Academic Project shall not be counted.
  • Experience in relevant field only shall be considered; HCL's decision on relevance is final.
  • Candidates from private organizations must be from companies with turnover of Rs. 250 Crores or more.
  • Selected candidates must execute a Service Agreement Bond of Rs. 3 Lakhs and serve a minimum of 3 years.
  • Government / CPSE employees must route their application through proper channel or submit NOC at the time of interview.
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates must submit OBC-NCL certificate issued on or after 01-04-2026.
  • EWS candidates must submit EWS certificate valid for the year 2026-27.
  • Category once filled/declared in the online application will not be changed.
  • All amendments/corrigendum will be communicated only through the HCL website - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Exact percentage of marks should be mentioned; rounding off of marks should not be done.
  • Email ID and mobile number provided in the application must remain active till recruitment is complete.
  • Canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Kolkata only.
  • HCL is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourages workplace diversity.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) has officially announced the General Manager in Finance & Accounts Recruitment 2026 with 15 openings. The detailed notification was published on the official BSNL website. Candidates who meet the age criteria of up to 50 years and hold a Chartered Accountant (CA), Cost & Management Accountant (CMA), MBA (Finance), or PG Diploma in Management (Finance) qualification can now submit their online applications. The application process will remain open until 24-06-2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Recruitment 2026 - Overview

Company Name Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL)
Post Name General Manager in Finance & Accounts
No of Posts 15
Advt No BSNLCO-11/12(13)/1/2026-RECTT-CO
Salary Rs. 2,25,000/- per month
Qualification Chartered Accountant (CA), Cost & Management Accountant (CMA), MBA (Finance), or PG Diploma in Management (Finance)
Age Limit Maximum age limit is 50 years
Last Date 24-06-2026
Apply Mode Online
Job Type Contract

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF

Candidates should download and read the BSNL Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below.

Download BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Job 2026 Notification PDF

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Vacancy Details 2026

Post Name Total Posts
General Manager in Finance & Accounts 15
Total 15

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Eligibility Criteria 2026

  • General Manager in Finance & Accounts:
    • Chartered Accountant (CA)
    • Cost & Management Accountant (CMA)
    • MBA (Finance) from a recognized university/institution
    • PG Diploma in Management (Finance) from a recognized university/institution
  • Experience: More than 15 years of post-qualification work experience in Executive/ Management grade in the Finance and Accounts domain including at least 5 years in Senior Executive/Managerial level of DGM and above, in listed companies or high-turnover firms with 3 years average turnover of Rs.3000 Crores or above supported by audited financial statements or credible documentary evidence.

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Application Fee 2026

Category Fee
All Rs. 1000/-

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Age Limit 2026

  • Maximum age limit is 50 years as on the closing date of submission of application.
  • No age relaxation details are provided in the official notification.

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Salary / Stipend 2026

  • Monthly remuneration: Rs. 2,25,000/- (Rupees Two Lakh Twenty Five Thousand only)
  • Annual increment: 5% (five percent)
  • Consolidated remuneration, no Dearness Allowance or other additional allowances.
  • Entitled to a residential Service Telephone Connection and a vehicle for official purposes.
  • Ineligible for further facilities, including residential accommodation or medical reimbursement.
  • No entitlement to pensionary benefits or post-retirement medical facilities.
  • No pay protection, financial benefit or service-related advantage from previous employment.

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

Event Date
Start of Online Registration 09 June 2026
End of Online Registration 24 June 2026
Start of Application Editing Period 25 June 2026
End of Application Editing Period 28 June 2026
Last Date to Apply 24 June 2026

BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Selection Process 2026

  • Shortlisting based on age, experience, and qualifications (Stage-I)
  • Group Discussion and Presentation (Stage-II)
  • Interview

How to Apply

Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the BSNL General Manager in Finance & Accounts Recruitment 2026.

  • Applications must be submitted online on or before 24-06-2026.
  • To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
  • Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of BSNL to complete the application.
  • Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
  • Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
  • Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
  • Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
  • Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 899+ active job listings.

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category

Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is OnlyJobs?

OnlyJobs is a trusted Indian job and exam information portal. Find latest sarkari job vacancies, exam results, admit cards, online forms at OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs.

Q2: How can I check the latest Sarkari jobs at OnlyJobs?

You can check the latest Sarkari jobs on OnlyJobs by visiting the latest jobs page, scanning the newest recruitment listings, and clicking the job title to see full details such as eligibility, application date, and official notification.

Q3: Does OnlyJobs provide सरकारी नौकरी updates?

Yes, OnlyJobs shares daily updates on सरकारी नौकरी, including vacancies from Railway, SSC, Banking, UPSC, Police, Army, and state-level recruitments.

Q4: Can I find admit cards and answer keys on OnlyJobs?

Yes, the website also publishes admit cards, answer keys, syllabus, cut-off marks, and other important exam-related notifications.Result notification service.

Q5: Is OnlyJobs an official government website?

No, OnlyJobs is a private information portal. It collects and publishes recruitment and exam updates from official government sources for public convenience.

Q6: Is this the official OnlyJobs website?

No. OnlyJobs is a private information portal, not a government website. All results, admit cards, and notifications link to their respective official government portals such as ssc.nic.in, upsc.gov.in, rrbcdg.gov.in, and ibps.in. OnlyJobs has been providing this aggregation service since 2011.

Q7: How do I get OnlyJobs alerts on my phone?

Download the OnlyJobs mobile app from Store, join the official WhatsApp Channel, or subscribe to the Telegram group. You will receive instant push notifications whenever a new result, admit card, or recruitment notification is published.

Q8: How can I check my OnlyJobs 2026?

Visit OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs daily. Find your exam in the Latest Results section, click the link, and you will be taken to the official government portal. Enter your Roll Number or Registration ID to view your result.

Delhi Police Head Constable Cut Off 2026 Released - Check Expected & Final Marks

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has officially released the cut off marks for Delhi Police Head Constable (Ministerial) recruitment 2026. Candidates who appeared in the written examination can now check their category-wise qualifying marks through the official portal. The cut off has been determined based on factors like total vacancies, number of applicants, exam difficulty level, and reservation policy.

Delhi Police Head Constable Cut Off 2026 - Quick Overview

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC) for Delhi Police
Post Name Head Constable (Ministerial)
Total Posts 509
Exam Type Written Examination (CBT)
Cut off Release Date 5th June 2026
Exam Date 7th to 12th January 2026
Cut Off Status ✓ Released
Selection Process Based on Merit & Cut Off Marks (CBT → PE&MT/DV/Trade Test)
Official Website ssc.gov.in
Download Cut Off PDF Click Here to Download (via Result PDF)

Category-Wise Delhi Police Head Constable Cut Off Marks 2026

The qualifying marks vary for different categories as per government reservation norms. Here are the official cut-off marks (normalized) for candidates qualified for the next stage (PE&MT/DV/Trade Test).

Male (Open) Candidates:

Category Cut Off Marks Candidates Available
UR 82.68 909
OBC 82.23 1,415
EWS 82.45 674
SC 75.80 947
ST 75.23 183
OH 54.29 160

Female (Open) Candidates:

Category Cut Off Marks Candidates Available
UR 75.97 515
OBC 74.78 773
EWS 75.97 379
SC 65.79 515
ST 66.18 109
OH 30.70 51

Male (ESM) Candidates: Lower cut-offs apply (e.g., UR/OBC ~54.24, others ~30).

How to Check Delhi Police Head Constable Cut Off Marks 2026 Online?

Follow these simple steps to download the official cut off notification:

Step 1: Visit the official website at ssc.gov.in

Step 2: Look for the "Latest Notices" or "Results" section on the homepage

Step 3: Click on "Delhi Police Head Constable (Ministerial) Result 2026" link

Step 4: The PDF will open in a new window - download and save it for future reference

Step 5: Check your category-wise qualifying marks and compare with your obtained score

What Happens After Cut Off Declaration?

Once the cut off is released, candidates who score equal to or above the qualifying marks will be shortlisted for the next round. The selection process typically includes:

• Physical Endurance & Measurement Test (PE&MT): Physical standards and efficiency test

• Document Verification: Submission of original certificates

• Trade/Skill Test: Typing/Computer proficiency (if applicable)

• Final Merit List: Based on overall performance in all stages

SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Scorecard

Quick Summary: Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026 on 04 June 2026 at official portal ssc.gov.in. Candidates can check their result and qualifying status for the next stage (PET/PST for CRPF posts). Check merit list, cutoff marks, and further selection process below.

The Staff Selection Commission has officially published the Paper-II Result for Junior Hindi Translator & related posts (04 June 2026). Candidates who appeared in the Paper-II (Descriptive) Exam held on 14 December 2025 can now verify their qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026 including direct download links, merit list, cutoff marks, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

SSC JHT 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Post Name Junior Hindi Translator (JHT), Senior Hindi Translator & Others
Result Type Paper-II (Tier-2) Result
Result Declaration Date 04 June 2026
Paper-II Exam Conducted On 14 December 2025
Selection Stages Paper-I → Paper-II → PET/PST (CRPF) → Document Verification → Final Merit
Official Website https://ssc.gov.in/

Check Your Result Now: Download SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026

How to Check SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026?

Candidates can download their SSC JHT Paper-II Result 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: ssc.gov.in
  2. Click on the “Results” section on the homepage
  3. Select “JHT” or “Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2025”
  4. Download the Paper-II Result PDF
  5. Search your Roll Number to check qualifying status

SSC JHT Paper-II Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The SSC JHT Paper-II merit list 2026 contains details of candidates shortlisted for the next stage (PET/PST).

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Number of qualified candidates
  • Category (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Marks in Paper-II
  • Qualifying Status
  • Shortlist for PET/PST (CRPF)

Types of Merit Lists Released:

  1. Overall Merit List: Combined qualified candidates
  2. Category-Wise Merit List: Separate lists for reserved categories
  3. CRPF Specific List: For Sub Inspector (Hindi Translator) PET/PST

SSC JHT Paper-II Scorecard 2026 - Information Breakdown

Your SSC JHT Paper-II scorecard 2026 will display the following information:

Candidate Name As per application
Roll Number Unique identification number
Paper-II Exam Date 14 December 2025
Marks Obtained Paper-II scores
Qualifying Status Qualified/Not Qualified
Next Stage PET/PST for CRPF

Important Instructions for Qualified Candidates

  • Download and save the result PDF immediately
  • Take printouts for future reference
  • Prepare for PET/PST Physical Tests
  • Keep all documents ready for verification
  • Submit Option-cum-Preference form on time
  • Verify details and report discrepancies immediately
  • Regularly check ssc.gov.in for PET schedule

What is Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026?

The Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi has released the Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) recruitment for 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches under the Agnipath Scheme for unmarried Indian male candidates with AICTE-recognised 3-year diplomas in engineering/electrical trades. Key facts:

  • Total vacancies: To be decided based on service requirement (trade-wise vacancies to be notified)
  • Streams/trades: Engineering (Marine Engg & Systems, Mechanical, Production/Automobile, Refrigeration & AC, Power Plant, Mechatronics, Metallurgy, Instrumentation, Electronics Instrumentation & Control, Industrial/Production) and Electrical (Electrical, E&C, Instrumentation Technology, Electronics & Control)
  • Pay package: ₹30,000/month (Year 1) to ₹40,000/month (Year 4); Seva Nidhi corpus ₹10.04 Lakh on completion
  • Age limit: 01 Dec 2004 – 31 May 2009 for 01/2027 batch; 01 May 2005 – 31 Oct 2009 for 02/2027 batch
  • Qualification: 10th pass with minimum 50% and a 3-year Diploma (AICTE-recognised) with minimum 50% in relevant trade
  • Application window: 05 June 2026 – 29 June 2026 (online at joinindiannavy.gov.in)
  • Selection process: Stage I INET (computer-based), Stage II PFT + written exam + recruitment medical, Pre-enrolment medical at INS Chilka
  • Exam: INET 2/2026 tentatively in Aug 2026 (100 MCQs, 1 hour, bilingual, negative marking 0.25)
  • Application fee: ₹550 + 18% GST (online); certain candidates (sponsored/NCC 'C') may be exempted
  • Service duration: 4 years under Agnipath Scheme (Navy Act 1957); up to 25% may be enrolled in Regular cadre based on performance
  • Other benefits: Risk/hardship allowances, non-contributory life insurance cover ₹48 Lakh, death/disability compensation, medical facilities; no pension/gratuity

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi has officially released the advertisement for Agniveer (Apprentice) - 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch under the Agnipath Scheme. The notification covers complete details on eligibility, age criteria, pay package, Seva Nidhi, selection process, medical standards, and general instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Indian Navy website.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Post-wise Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date. Only unmarried Indian male candidates are eligible to apply. All qualifications must be from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institutes.

Agniveer (Apprentice) - Engineering & Electrical Streams

Criteria Details
Essential Educational Qualification Passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India AND completed a 3-year Diploma course from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with minimum 50% marks in aggregate in any of the qualifying diploma trades (Engineering or Electrical as applicable).
Marital Status Only unmarried male candidates are eligible. Candidates must give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers will not be permitted to marry during their entire 4-year tenure. A candidate will be dismissed from service if he marries during tenure or is found to be already married despite giving an unmarried certificate.
Nationality Indian male citizens only.
Preferential Proficiency in sports, swimming, and extra-curricular activities is desirable.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online

The Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 online application process has officially commenced for candidates seeking enrollment in the 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batches. Eligible applicants can submit their application forms through the official Indian Navy recruitment portal during the prescribed registration period. Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

The Application Window (Stage I – INET 2/2026) opens on 05 June 2026 and will remain active until 29 June 2026 (05:00 PM). After the application period ends, the Indian Navy will provide a Correction Window on 06 July 2026, allowing candidates to rectify permissible errors in their submitted application forms. Applicants should carefully verify all personal, educational, and communication details before final submission.

To apply online, candidates must first register on the official Indian Navy recruitment website using a valid email ID and mobile number. After registration, they need to fill in the online application form, upload the required documents, and submit the form successfully. It is recommended to keep scanned copies of the photograph, signature, educational certificates, and identity proof ready before starting the application process.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Stage 1 - INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test)

Separate INET exams will be conducted for Electrical and Engineering entries. INET 2/2026 will be conducted in August 2026 with SSC as Exam Partner.

Parameter Details
Mode Computer-based
Total Questions 100 questions of 1 mark each
Language Bilingual - Hindi & English
Type Objective / Multiple Choice
Sections English & General Awareness, Science, Mathematics, and Diploma (Trade Specific)
Duration 1 Hour
Negative Marking 0.25 marks deducted per wrong answer
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate. Indian Navy reserves the right to determine pass marks in each section and in aggregate.
Centre Allocation At the discretion of the Indian Navy; state preference to be indicated by candidate

Stage 2 - Physical Fitness Test (PFT)

Qualifying in PFT is mandatory for selection. Candidates undergoing PFT do so at their own risk.

Gender 1.6 Km Run Squats (Uthak Baithak) Push-ups Bent Knee Sit-ups
Male 06 min 30 sec 20 15 15

Stage 2 - Written Examination

Written exam is conducted only for candidates who qualify PFT. Two separate exams are conducted for Engineering and Electrical streams.

Parameter Details
Total Questions 100 questions of 1 mark each
Language Bilingual - Hindi & English
Type Objective / Multiple Choice
Sections English & General Awareness, Science, Mathematics, and Diploma (Trade Specific)
Duration 1 Hour
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate

Stage 2 - Recruitment Medical Examination

Conducted for all candidates who qualify PFT. Candidates found medically unfit may appeal within 05 days at a designated Military Hospital allocated by the Indian Navy. Medical fitness certificates from hospitals other than designated Military Hospitals will not be considered. No second appeal is permissible.

Stage 3 - Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka

Pre-enrolment medical formalities of all selected candidates will be conducted at INS Chilka, Odisha. Induction is subject to qualifying in Pre-Enrolment Medical Examination. Candidates not qualifying will be rejected.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Emoluments / Pay Package

Year Customised Package (Monthly) In Hand - 70% (Monthly) Contribution to Agniveer Corpus Fund - 30% (Monthly) Contribution to Corpus Fund by GoI (Monthly)
1st Year ₹30,000 ₹21,000 ₹9,000 ₹9,000
2nd Year ₹33,000 ₹23,100 ₹9,900 ₹9,900
