4th Year ₹40,000 ₹28,000 ₹12,000 ₹12,000
Total in Agniveer Corpus Fund (Seva Nidhi) ₹5.02 Lakh ₹5.02 Lakh

Total Seva Nidhi on completion of 4-year tenure: ₹10.04 Lakhs (Agniveer's contribution + matching GoI contribution). Additional benefits include:

  • Risk, hardship, dress and travel allowances
  • Non-contributory Life Insurance Cover of ₹48 Lakh for the duration of engagement
  • Death Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 Lakh (attributable to service) in addition to insurance cover
  • Disability Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 / ₹25 / ₹15 Lakh based on 100% / 75% / 50% disability
  • 30 days leave per year; sick leave as per medical advice
  • Medical facilities at service hospitals and CSD provisions
  • No gratuity or pensionary benefits applicable

How to Apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://joinindiannavy.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. Applications can also be uploaded through Common Service Centres (CSC) across the country at a fee of ₹60 + GST (optional facility).

Steps to Apply Online:

  • Visit https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Click on "Register" → Register as AVR (Apprentice).
  • After successful registration, click on "Login" → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26 → Proceed to apply.
  • Fill in all details carefully. Incorrect declaration of information identified at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Upload a recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026), size 10 KB to 50 KB; front portrait in light background without head gear (except Sikhs). Date of photograph must be clearly written at the bottom.
  • A live photograph will be captured through the webcam during application form filling.
  • Pay the examination fee of ₹550/- + 18% GST online via net banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI.
  • Give state preference for allocation of INET exam centre. Choice of centre cannot be changed once selected or allotted by the Indian Navy.
  • Candidates will be provided a post-closing correction window. An additional correction window will be activated post INET exam for 2 days.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Matriculation Certificate and Marksheet (proof of date of birth and educational qualification)
  • Diploma Certificate and Consolidated Marksheet from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute
  • Domicile Certificate
  • NCC 'C' Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through DG NCC
  • Sponsorship Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through CABS
  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026)

Documents to be Produced at INS Chilka at the time of Induction:

  • All original certificates as submitted/uploaded during online application
  • Self-attested copies of all certificates
  • Original NCC 'C' Certificate / Sponsorship Certificate (if held)
  • Police Verification Form duly verified by the Superintendent of Police of respective jurisdiction (from place of domicile or place of residence)
  • Call-up letter / Admit Card

Apply Online at: https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Register → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26

Call-up Letters cum Admit Card for Stage I - INET 2/2026 will be available for download from https://joinindiannavy.gov.in approximately one week before the online examination. No call-up letter will be sent by post. Only Electronic mode of communication will be used for contacting candidates.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Important Instructions

  • Only unmarried Indian male candidates are eligible. Agniveers cannot marry during their entire 4-year tenure; violation will result in dismissal from service.
  • Agniveers are enrolled under the Navy Act 1957 for a period of 4 years. Indian Navy is not obliged to retain Agniveers beyond the engagement period.
  • Up to 25% of each specific batch of Agniveers may be offered permanent enrolment in the Indian Navy as Sailors (Regular Cadre) / Artificers based on performance and organisational requirements. Selection for further enrolment is entirely at the discretion of the Indian Navy; Agniveers have no right to be selected.
  • Agniveers will not be permitted to seek release at their own request prior to completion of the engagement period.
  • Agniveers are not governed by Naval Pension Regulations/Rules and are not entitled to gratuity for the engagement period.
  • Agniveers will not be eligible for Ex-Servicemen status.
  • Candidates declaring incorrect domicile/gender and other details shall not be permitted to appear in the recruitment and their candidature will be cancelled.
  • Candidates reporting 30 minutes after the reporting time for Stage I - INET shall not be allowed to appear in the examination.
  • A candidate's selection pertaining to a particular batch is valid for that batch only. Qualified candidates whose names do not appear in the merit list cannot claim admission for the next batch.
  • Mobile phones or any other communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises. Infringement will entail disciplinary action including ban from future examinations.
  • Candidates should not submit multiple applications. If more than one application is received, candidature will be cancelled.
  • Minimum height standard is 157 cms. Permanent body tattoos are only permitted on the inner face of forearms (inside of elbow to wrist) and on the reversed side of palm/back (dorsal) side of hand. Tattoos on any other part of the body are not acceptable.
  • Candidates are advised to get their ears cleaned for wax and tartar removed from teeth prior to medical examination.
  • Visual Standards: Uncorrected Vision 6/12, 6/12 | Corrected Vision 6/6, 6/6 | Colour Perception: CP PASS.
  • No enquiry will be entertained regarding this recruitment/enrolment after a period of six months post completion of the recruitment cycle for the respective batch.
  • The terms and conditions given in the advertisement are guidelines only and are subject to change without notice. Further, orders issued by the Government as amended from time to time will apply for selected candidates.

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) posts through Direct Recruitment from Open Market! Published on 26-05-2026 vide Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO, this new recruitment drive offers 100 openings for eligible candidates across 21 BSNL Circles. Applicants within the 20 to 30 years age group and possessing a B.E. / B.Tech. (Telecom / Electronics / Radio / Computer / Electrical / IT / Instrumentation) or M.Sc. (Electronics / Computer Science) from a recognized University can apply. The online link has been activated, and applications can be submitted from 04-06-2026 to 03-07-2026. Find all details about the BSNL JTO (Telecom) Recruitment 2026, including eligibility, online application, and official notification PDF, below.

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 100 vacancies have been announced for Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) – Direct Recruitment posts. These are distributed across 21 BSNL Circles (Circle-wise) with category-wise breakup as per Central Government guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/Ex-Serviceman candidates. JTO is a Circle Cadre post; selected candidates will be allotted a Circle based on their preference, merit, and vacancies available. Candidates may give preferences for up to all 21 Circles. Opting for more circles improves the chances of selection.

Reservation as per Central Government guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/EWS/Ex-Serviceman. Reservation for PwBD is horizontal within overall vacancies. No change in category will ordinarily be allowed after submission of online application.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description
Deaf or Hard of Hearing (HI) – Category (b) Hard of Hearing / Deaf as defined under RPwD Act 2016, with not less than 40% disability as certified by certifying authority. Benefit of reservation may be given to PwBDs with Permanent Disability Certificates only.
Locomotor Disability including Cerebral Palsy, leprosy cured, dwarfism, acid attack victims and muscular dystrophy – Category (c) Locomotor Disability as defined under RPwD Act 2016, with not less than 40% disability. Forms for Certificate of Disability may be same as prescribed under RPwD Act 2016 / RPwD Rules 2017 as amended from time to time.

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (03-07-2026). Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision. The qualification must have been completed as on the closing date of receipt of application from a University recognized under UGC Act, 1956.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification (as on 03-07-2026) Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt. / State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: (1) Telecommunications, (2) Electronics, (3) Radio, (4) Computer, (5) Electrical, (6) Information Technology (IT), (7) Instrumentation
OR
M.Sc. (Electronics) / M.Sc. (Computer Science).

Note: Candidates possessing higher qualifications viz. M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible.
Colour Vision / Binocular Vision The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision.
OBC (NCL) Certificate validity OBC (NCL) certificate should have been issued on or after 01.04.2026 by the competent authority, containing date of issue, name of caste spelled exactly as in Central Government list, and the Village/Town where the candidate ordinarily resides.
EWS Certificate validity Income and Asset Certificate (EWS) should have been issued on or after 01.04.2026 by the competent authority in prescribed format (as per DoPT OM No. 36039/1/2019–Estt (Res) dated 31/01/2019).
Nationality Indian Nationals only.

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and not exceed 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application (03-07-2026). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved / EWS / OBC (CL) candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Central Government guidelines.

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the prescribed examination fee through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL External Exam portal (www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in). Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection. Additionally, a challenge/grievance window fee of Rs. 400/- per question/answer key is charged post-examination (non-refundable).

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT). All candidates will be given a score based on their performance in the CBT. A final merit list will be prepared based on total marks obtained across all three sections. Selected candidates will go for document verification and medical examination. Appearance in the final merit list does not confer any right for appointment — a final offer letter will be issued only after successful completion of all pre-appointment formalities. BSNL reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify/alter the recruitment/selection process at any stage without notice.

Stage 1 – Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT)

Section Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
I Engineering Stream Section – I 50 200 3 Hours (Single shift, all sections combined)
II Engineering Stream Section – II 50 200
III General Ability Test Section – III 20 80
Total 120 480 3 Hours

Marking Scheme: For each correct answer: +4 Marks. For each wrong answer: -1 Mark (Negative Marking). The standard of engineering papers will be that of Engineering Degree Examination of an Indian University. In the General Ability Test, special attention will be paid to assess the candidate's capacity for general awareness, at the level expected of an Engineering Graduate. If the examination is held in multiple sessions, normalization of marks will be adopted. Minimum Qualifying Marks: For UR/OBC (CL) candidates — minimum 40% in each section and minimum 50% overall across all three sections combined. For SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates — minimum 35% in each section and minimum 45% overall. Candidates not meeting sectional or overall minimum qualifying marks will not be included in the merit list. The final merit list will be prepared based on total marks in all three sections combined, subject to minimum qualifying marks criteria being met.

Stage 2 – Document Verification

Candidates in the declared merit list will be called for document verification. Date and venue will be intimated in due course. All original certificates along with self-attested photocopies must be produced at the time of document verification.

Stage 3 – Medical Examination

Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to their passing the Company's Medical Examination as per standards laid down by BSNL. Candidates who are already employed elsewhere must resign from their current organization only after being declared medically fit. Details will be provided on BSNL website www.bsnl.co.in.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online

The online application process for BSNL Junior Telecom Officer (JTO) Recruitment 2026 has started on 04 June 2026. Interested and eligible candidates who possess a B.Tech/B.E or M.Sc qualification can submit their applications through the official BSNL website. A total of 100 Junior Telecom Officer vacancies have been announced across India under this recruitment drive.

The online application link has been activated on the official portal and candidates can complete the registration process before the closing date. Applicants are advised to carefully read the official notification, verify their eligibility, and keep all required documents ready before filling out the online application form.

The last date to apply online for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 is 03 July 2026. Candidates should submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid any last-minute technical issues or server congestion.

  • Apply Online for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Exam Pattern & Syllabus

The examination is a Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT) of 3 hours duration consisting of three sections. Sections I and II cover Engineering Stream subjects at B.E./B.Tech. degree level, and Section III covers General Ability. The detailed syllabus topic-wise is given below.

Section I – Engineering Stream (Topics)

Topic Key Subtopics
1. Materials and Components Structure and properties of Electronic Engineering materials; Conductors, Semiconductors and Insulators; Magnetic, Ferroelectric, Piezoelectric, Ceramic, Optical and Superconducting materials; Passive components and characteristics – Resistors, Capacitors, Inductors; Ferrites, Quartz crystal, Ceramic resonators; Electromagnetic and Electromechanical components.
2. Physical Electronics, Electron Devices and ICs Electrons and holes in semiconductors; Carrier Statistics; Mechanics of current flow in a semi-conductor; Hall effect; Junction theory; Different types of diodes and their characteristics; Bipolar Junction transistor; Field effect transistors; Power switching devices like SCRs, CTOs, power MOSFETs; Basics of ICs-bipolar, MOS and CMOS types; Basics of Opto Electronics.
3. Network Theory Network analysis techniques; Network theorem; Transient and steady state sinusoidal response; Transmission criteria – delay and rise time, Elmore's and other definitions; Effect of cascading; Elements of network synthesis.
4. Electromagnetic Theory Transmission lines – basic theory, standing waves, matching applications, microstrip lines; Basics of waveguides and resonators; Elements of antenna theory.
5. Electronic Measurements and Instrumentation Basic concepts, standards and error analysis; Measurements of basic electrical quantities and parameters; Electronic measuring instruments and their principles of working – analog and digital, comparison, characteristics, applications; Transducers; Electronic measurements of non-electrical quantities like temperature, pressure, humidity; Basics of telemetry for industrial use.
6. Power Electronics Power Semiconductor devices; Thyristor; Power transistor; MOSFETs; Characteristics and operation; AC to DC convertors; 1-Phase and 3-phase DC to DC Convertors; AC regulators; Thyristor controlled reactors; Switched capacitor networks; Inverters – Single-phase and 3-phase; Pulse width modulation; Sinusoidal modulation with uniform sampling; Switched mode power supplies.

Section II – Engineering Stream (Topics)

Topic Key Subtopics
1. Analog Electronic Circuits Transistor biasing and stabilization; Small Signal analysis; Power amplifiers; Frequency response; Wide band techniques; Feedback amplifiers; Tuned amplifiers; Oscillators; Rectifiers and power supplies; Operational Amplifier; Other linear integrated circuits and applications; Pulse shaping circuits and waveform generators.
2. Digital Electronic Circuits Transistor as a switching element; Boolean algebra; Simplification of Boolean functions; Karnaugh Map and applications; IC Logic gates and their characteristics; IC logic families – DTL, TTL, ECL, NMOS, PMOS and CMOS gates and their comparison; Combinational logic circuits; Half adder, full adder; Digital Compartor; Multiplexer; Demultiplexer; ROM and their applications; Flip-flops – R-S, J-K, D and T types; Different types of counters and registers; Waveform generators; A/D and D/A converters; Semiconductor memories.
3. Control Systems Transient and steady state response of control systems; Effect of feedback on stability and sensitivity; Root locus techniques; Frequency response analysis; Concepts of gain and phase margins; Constant-M and Constant-N Nichol's Chart; Approximation of transient response from Constant-N Nichol's Chart; Approximation of transient response from closed loop frequency response; Design of Control Systems; Compensators; Industrial controllers.
4. Communication Systems Basic information theory; Modulation and detection in analogue and digital systems; Sampling and data reconstruction; Quantization & Coding; Time division and frequency division multiplexing; Equalisation; Optical Communication – in free space & fibre optic; Propagation of signals at HF, VHF, UHF and microwave frequency; Satellite communication.
5. Microwave Engineering Microwave Tubes and solid state devices; Microwave generation and amplifiers; Waveguides and other Microwave Components and Circuits; Microstrip circuits; Microwave antennas; Microwave Measurements; MASERS; LASERS; Microwave Propagation; Microwave Communication Systems – terrestrial and satellite based.
6. Computer Engineering Number Systems; Data representation; Programming; Elements of a high level programming language PASCAL/C; Use of basic data structures; Fundamentals of computer architecture processor design; Control unit design; Memory organization; I/O System Organization; Personal computers and their typical uses.
7. Microprocessors Microprocessor architecture – Instruction set and simple assembly language programming; Interfacing for memory and I/O; Applications of Microprocessors in Telecommunications and power system.

Section III – General Ability Test

Topic Key Subtopics
General Ability Test General English comprehension and understanding; Current Events and General Knowledge; Everyday observation and experience in scientific aspects as may be expected of an educated person; Events and developments in Telecommunications; History of India; Geography. Questions will be of a nature that can be answered without special study by an educated person.

Examination Centre Cities (27 Cities – ANNEXURE-C)

The examination will be held in 27 cities/centres across India. Candidates must choose only one Examination City/Centre at the time of registration. The exam centre may be any of the 27 cities or a nearby city depending upon number of candidates. The 27 cities are: Port Blair, Vijayawada, Guwahati, Patna, Chennai, Raipur, Ahmedabad, Ambala, Shimla, Jammu, Ranchi, Bangalore, Thiruvananthapuram, Kolkata, Bhopal, Mumbai, Shillong, Dimapur, New Delhi, Bhubaneshwar, Chandigarh, Jaipur, Gangtok, Hyderabad, Lucknow, Meerut, Dehradun. No request for change of examination centre will be entertained after submission.

BSNL JTO Syllabus and Exam Pattern 2026

BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 - Bond, Training & Probation

All selected candidates for JTO(T)-DR will execute a Bond of Rs. 6 (Six) Lakhs in the format specified by BSNL to serve BSNL for a period of at least FIVE YEARS from the date of appointment as Junior Telecom Officer. Period of probation is Two Years. All JTO(T)-DR candidates must successfully undergo training for such period as may be specified by BSNL. Training details: JTO Phase-I training: 10 weeks; Field Training: 4 weeks; JTO Phase-II training (Specialisation course): 6 weeks. During training, the Direct Recruit JTO will be entitled to stipend (training allowance) as decided by BSNL from time to time. Seniority on appointment in JTO(T) cadre will be determined on the basis of 60% weightage of marks obtained in the DR Examination and 40% weightage of marks obtained in pre-appointment Phase-I training. In case of equal marks, seniority will be determined by date of birth (older candidate will be senior).

Competition Level Analysis for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026

The competition level for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high, as only 100 posts are available across 21 Circles for an All-India open market recruitment targeting Engineering graduates in Electronics/Telecom/CS/IT and related disciplines. Karnataka (26 seats), Kerala (15 seats), and Maharashtra (16 seats) will attract the highest number of serious applicants since they have the most vacancies and are also home to a large pool of engineering graduates. Circles like Rajasthan, Sikkim, Tamilnadu, and Uttarakhand with only 1 seat each will be the most competitive on a per-seat basis, where even a fraction of a mark can decide selection.

The 480-mark, 3-hour CBT with sectional minimum qualifying marks makes it critical to maintain balance across all three sections — candidates who neglect the General Ability section or fail to meet the per-section cut-off (40%/35%) will be eliminated regardless of their total score. The Rs. 2,000 exam fee and the mandatory 5-year bond (Rs. 6 Lakhs) will filter out casual applicants, leaving mostly serious engineering graduates. The Circle preference system rewards candidates who opt for more circles, as it gives BSNL flexibility to allocate based on merit and availability.

Who should NOT apply for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not hold a B.E./B.Tech. in any of the 7 specified disciplines (Telecom, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, IT, Instrumentation) or M.Sc. (Electronics/Computer Science) from a recognized University.
  • Candidates who cannot distinguish principal colours or do not have binocular vision — this is a mandatory eligibility condition for the JTO(T) post.
  • Candidates above 30 years of age as on 03-07-2026 (for UR/EWS/OBC-CL category) who do not qualify for any applicable relaxation.
  • Candidates not willing to serve anywhere in India or be transferred to any BSNL Circle — the post carries a liability to be transferred anywhere in India in the interest of service.
  • Candidates not willing to execute the bond of Rs. 6 Lakhs to serve BSNL for at least 5 years from the date of appointment.
  • Candidates who have entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a living spouse, or who having a living spouse, have entered into marriage with any person — such candidates are not eligible for appointment.
  • Candidates from outside India (Non-Indian Nationals) — only Indian Nationals are eligible.
  • OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates who wish to apply under OBC NCL reservation — Creamy Layer OBC candidates must indicate category as UR.

Also Read: Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online

Common mistakes / What to check before applying BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026?

  • Not selecting enough Circle preferences during registration — candidates are strongly advised to select all 21 Circles in order of preference to maximise the chance of being selected based on merit. Opting for fewer Circles restricts candidature to only those opted Circles.
  • Uploading photograph or signature outside the prescribed size/format: photo must be in JPG/JPEG format, 20kb–50kb with white background; signature must be in JPG/JPEG format, 10kb–20kb only.
  • Using an incorrect or inactive mobile number/email ID — OTP will be sent to the registered mobile number for completing the registration process; all further communications will be sent only to these contacts.
  • Submitting multiple applications — in case of multiple registrations, only the latest application number will be considered as final and older applications will be rejected.
  • Not retaining the BSNL Registration Slip and Admit Card printout — no documents including copy of Registration Slip are to be sent to BSNL unless specifically asked.
  • Attempting to change the Examination Centre or discipline after submission — no such requests will be entertained.
  • Candidates already employed in Government Departments/Public Sector Undertakings/Autonomous Bodies must produce a "No Objection Certificate" from their present employer at the time of document verification — failure to produce this will affect appointment.
  • Not noting the Rs. 400/- challenge fee per question/answer key for post-exam grievance submission — this fee is non-refundable and must be paid online.
  • Underestimating negative marking — for every wrong answer, 1 mark is deducted. Avoid guessing randomly; unattempted questions carry no penalty.
  • Not checking sectional cut-offs — candidates must meet the minimum qualifying marks in each individual section (40% for UR/OBC-CL, 35% for reserved categories) in addition to the overall minimum, or they will not be considered for the merit list regardless of total marks.

How to Apply for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online only through the BSNL External Exam portal (www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in). No manual/paper application will be entertained. Candidates should keep the following ready before starting the registration process: valid mobile number and email ID for OTP, scanned photograph, signature, Class 10th certificate, Degree Certificate, Consolidated Mark Sheet, Photo ID proof, Category/Disability Certificate (if applicable).

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph with white background in JPG/JPEG format (20kb – 50kb only)
  • Signature in JPG/JPEG format (10kb – 20kb only)
  • Class 10th Certificate (for verification of Date of Birth)
  • Degree Certificate of Essential Qualification
  • Consolidated Mark Sheet / transcript of essential qualification for all semesters or years
  • Photo identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Passport, Driving License, Voter ID Card)
  • Valid proof of reservation category as applicable (SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD)
  • Medical Certificate for PwBD (issued by Govt. Medical Board in prescribed format)

Documents to be Produced at Document Verification / Appointment:

  • Admit Card (downloaded from www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in using username & password) along with valid photo identity proof in original
  • All original educational qualification certificates and mark sheets
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Central Government format (for SC/ST candidates)
  • OBC (NCL) Certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 in prescribed Central Government format (for OBC NCL candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 in prescribed format (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate issued by Govt. Medical Board in prescribed format (for PwBD candidates)
  • "No Objection Certificate" from present employer (for candidates employed in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies)
  • Date of Birth proof (Matriculation / Secondary School Leaving Certificate or equivalent)

Apply Online at: https://www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in

For queries: Contact BSNL Corporate Office, New Delhi at [email protected]. BSNL does not seek payments of any kind other than the registration fee and post-exam grievance fee. Candidates are advised not to respond to unscrupulous advertisements; check authenticity only on www.bsnl.co.in.

Important Instructions for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Both internal BSNL employees and external (open market) candidates fulfilling the prescribed eligibility conditions are eligible to apply.
  • JTO is a Circle Cadre post; candidates will be allotted a Circle depending on vacancies, their preference, and merit. Options once exercised shall be final — change of Circle afterwards will not be allowed.
  • Candidates are strongly advised to select all 21 Circles in order of preference to maximise the possibility of selection. Selecting a limited number of Circles restricts candidature only to those opted Circles.
  • All selected candidates must execute a Bond of Rs. 6 Lakhs to serve BSNL for at least 5 years from the date of appointment as Junior Telecom Officer.
  • The admit card will be available only for download from the BSNL website — it will NOT be sent by post or email. Issuance of admit card does not confer any right for appointment.
  • After closing of registration period, an Editing Window will be opened for 7 days — candidates can edit selective fields only. No further editing will be allowed after the editing period.
  • Candidates applying for UR vacancy can also be SC/ST/EWS/OBC/Ex-Serviceman/BSNL employees subject to meeting all general eligibility standards — category relaxation in such cases cannot be availed.
  • Candidature is liable to be rejected at any stage if any information provided is found false or not in conformity with the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement.
  • No request for withdrawal of candidature will be entertained. Any dispute will be subject to the courts/tribunals having jurisdiction at New Delhi.
  • Canvassing in any form or influencing officials related to the selection/recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of the candidate.
  • Candidates who have a living spouse and have entered into another marriage, or who have married a person with a living spouse, are not eligible for appointment.
  • BSNL reserves the right to modify/amend any terms and conditions of this recruitment at any point of time. Dispute, if any, shall be within the jurisdiction of Delhi only.
  • BSNL Management reserves the right to cancel the recruitment exercise fully/partially at any stage at its discretion without notice.
  • BSNL does not seek payments of any kind other than the registration fee and post-exam grievance fee — candidates are advised to beware of fraudulent advertisements.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Kerala CSEB Cooperative Bank Recruitment 2026: The Cooperative Service Examination Board (CSEB), Kerala has released 7 official notifications on May 30, 2026, inviting online applications for multiple posts across cooperative banks and societies throughout Kerala. The advertisements cover posts ranging from Secretary and Assistant Secretary to Junior Clerk, Data Entry Operator, and System Administrator - across different grades of cooperative banks.

All eligible candidates must apply online through the CSEB official website www.cseb.kerala.gov.in on or before June 30, 2026. Selection will be based on an online/OMR-based written examination conducted by the Board, followed by interviews by the respective cooperative institutions. Read the complete post-wise details below before applying.

Exam Pattern

The CSEB written examination consists of 100 questions carrying 1 mark each (total 100 marks).

  • For each bank/society in the eligible list, candidates get a maximum of 20 marks allocated for the interview stage
  • To qualify for the interview, a candidate must score a minimum of 4 marks in the written exam (out of the 20 marks allocated per institution)
  • The actual interview cutoff is 16 marks out of 20

Exam Mode:

  • Posts 15, 16, 20, 21: Online (Computer-Based) Examination
  • Posts 17, 18, 19: OMR-Based (Offline) Examination

Exam Fee

All candidates must pay the examination fee along with 18% GST as per Government Order (S.O.(MS) No.17/2024/Co-op dated 13/08/2024).

SC / ST ₹50 ₹50 per additional institution

Important: Candidates can apply to more than one bank/society in a single advertisement. Each additional institution requires an additional fee of ₹50. Applications by post are not accepted - online submission only.

How to Apply for Kerala CSEB Recruitment 2026

  1. Visit the official CSEB website: www.cseb.kerala.gov.in
  2. New candidates: Complete One Time Registration (OTR) first. You can apply only after completing OTR.
  3. Already registered candidates: Log in with your existing profile and proceed to apply online.
  4. Select the advertisement number for the post you want to apply for.
  5. Read the full notification carefully before submitting.
  6. Fill in all required details accurately.
  7. Pay the examination fee online (with 18% GST).
  8. Submit the application and print a copy from the "My Application" section.
  9. Applications close on June 30, 2026.

Note: Postal applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Only online submissions through the CSEB portal are valid.

What Is CSEB Kerala? - Understanding the Recruitment Process

The Cooperative Service Examination Board (CSEB), Kerala, is the state body that conducts recruitment examinations for vacancies in Kerala's cooperative banks and societies. Unlike PSC (Public Service Commission) which recruits for government departments, CSEB specifically handles the cooperative sector.

The recruitment process works in two layers:

  1. CSEB conducts the written examination and prepares a ranked list of qualified candidates for each advertisement
  2. The individual cooperative bank/society conducts the interview from the shortlisted candidates and makes the final appointment

This means that after you clear the written exam, the specific bank where the vacancy exists will call you for an interview. Final appointment is made by that bank - you are an employee of the cooperative bank, not a Kerala Government employee. However, the salary scales, service conditions, and regulations are governed by the Kerala Cooperative Societies Act and rules.

Why Apply for a Kerala Cooperative Bank Job?

Many candidates overlook cooperative bank jobs while focusing only on PSC or central government posts. Here is why CSEB recruitment deserves serious consideration:

  • Stable, permanent employment governed by the Kerala Co-operative Societies Act
  • Pay scales competitive with state government Level equivalents for the same qualifications
  • Local posting - most cooperative banks post staff within the district or region, unlike state government transfers
  • Less competition than PSC exams - the candidate pool for CSEB exams is significantly smaller than for state-level PSC recruitments
  • Multiple banks in one advertisement - you can apply to multiple institutions in the same advt by paying an additional ₹50 per bank, increasing your selection chances significantly
  • Fresher-friendly - the Junior Clerk posts especially have no experience requirement

Tips for CSEB Written Exam Preparation

  • The 100-question, 100-mark written exam typically covers: General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Kerala-specific awareness, Cooperative law and principles, Mathematics/Reasoning, and English/Malayalam language
  • For Secretary and Assistant Secretary posts, expect questions on cooperative management, accounting principles, banking regulations, and the Kerala Cooperative Societies Act in depth
  • For Junior Clerk posts, the pattern is closer to a standard clerical aptitude test - reasoning, basic arithmetic, language comprehension
  • For System Administrator, expect technical questions on networking, OS administration, database management, and IT security alongside cooperative awareness
  • For DEO, expect typing/computer knowledge components alongside the written test
  • The minimum qualifying score is low (4/20 for interview eligibility) but the real competition is in clearing the cutoff that gets you called for an interview - typically much higher in popular banks. Focus on scoring in the top tier rather than just qualifying.

Can You Apply for Multiple Banks in the Same Advertisement?

Yes - and this is a significant strategic advantage. Unlike most government recruitment processes where you apply to one organization, the CSEB system allows a single candidate to apply to multiple cooperative banks under the same advertisement number. Each additional bank beyond the first requires an extra ₹50 exam fee.

For example, under Advt. 17/2026 (Junior Clerk – Super Grade), if you want to be considered for 3 banks, you pay: ₹150 (first bank) + ₹50 + ₹50 = ₹250 (+ 18% GST).

This is particularly useful for candidates who are flexible about work location within Kerala, as it dramatically increases the number of vacancies you are effectively competing for.

Sourced from official CSEB Kerala notifications Advt. No. 15/2026 to 21/2026, issued by the Cooperative Service Examination Board, Thiruvananthapuram, dated May 30, 2026.

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BOB recruitment notification 2026 for 5000 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The online application process starts on 19th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8th June 2026. Find Bank of Baroda Apprentice vacancies across all States/UTs on the official website http://bankofbaroda.bank.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda has officially released Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 5000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying. The notification PDF is available on the official Bank of Baroda website and BFSI SSC's website.

  • Download Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 5000 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Candidates must be registered on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) before applying.
Educational Qualification Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other Establishment in the past, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training with any Establishment.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
State/UT Application Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only and must select up to 3 preferred training locations/centres from those notified within that State/UT.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

General / EWS / OBC – Female / Transgender Candidates Rs. 600/- plus GST

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination, Document Verification, and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

Stage 1 - Online Examination

S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
2 Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 25 25 Hindi / English
3 Computer Knowledge 25 25 Hindi / English
4 General English 25 25 English
Total 100 100  

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera, microphone, and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for, at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.

Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:

  • Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:

  • Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
  • Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
  • Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda.
  • The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid.
  • The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC.
  • No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable.
  • Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage.
  • Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination.
  • Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses.
  • Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training.
  • Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable.
  • All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

How to Download RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026:

  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials:

    • Password / Date of Birth

  5. Step 5: Click on “Submit” button.
  6. Step 6: Your RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen.
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device.
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies).

Use this formula to estimate your score:

  4. Solution 4: Contact RBI helpdesk immediately

In this article, you'll find the Various recruitment details, including eligibility criteria, age limit, salary structure, selection process, email application procedure, and direct links to the official notification.

NKGSB Bank Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

  • Branch Managers: Graduate in any discipline, CAIIB/JAIIB preferred. Minimum 5 years to 7 years of branch banking experience in officer of Assistant Manager cadre, with branch leadership exposure.
  • Head IBD: Graduate in any discipline + Degree/Diploma in Trade Finance; CAIIB preferred. 15 years of banking experience in Executive cadre.
  • Asst. General Manager / Dy. General Manager - Operations: Graduate with relevant specialised banking experience. An experienced professional, to manage customer grievances, ensure regulatory compliance, and act as the primary liaison between the organization, the regulator (RBI), and the customer and responsible for resolving customer complaints. Will have Operations Dept., Clearing Dept., RTGS/Demat, Grievance Redressal Unit, Call Centre and CPC department responsibilities.
  • Asst. General Manager - Recovery: CAIIB & LLB graduate with relevant specialised banking experience. Senior professionals with proven track record in Recovery, NPA management and legal follow-up. Responsible for performing loss mitigation and recovery activities regarding delinquent or charged-off accounts in accordance with Bank and legal guidelines.

Category Fee
No application fee is mentioned in the official notification.

NKGSB Bank Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

  • Branch Managers: Upto 40 years.
  • Head IBD: Upto 50 years (relaxable in special cases).

How to Apply

Candidates who wish to apply for NKGSB Bank Various Posts Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.

  • Read the official notification carefully before applying.
  • Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
  • Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
  • Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
  • Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
  • Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 08-06-2026.
  • Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must possess the following educational qualifications as on the last date of application. Knowledge of Kannada is mandatory for all posts - candidates must be able to read, write, speak, and understand Kannada.

Sr. No. Post Name Educational Qualification
1 Manager / First Division Assistant 1. Must have passed a degree from any University established by law in India. 2. Must have the ability to read Kannada along with knowledge of Kannada language, writing, clear spoken Kannada, and ability to understand Kannada. 3. Must have knowledge of computer operation.
2 Second Division Assistant 1. Must have passed Second PUC or equivalent from a recognized institution, or currently studying. 2. Must have the ability to read Kannada along with knowledge of Kannada language, writing, clear spoken Kannada, and ability to understand Kannada. 3. Must have knowledge of computer operation.
3 Vehicle Driver 1. Must have passed SSLC; must be able to read Kannada as one language. 2. Must hold a valid legal driving license.
4 Junior Servant 1. Must have passed SSLC; must be able to read Kannada as one language.

Note:

  • If reserved category seats for Women / Rural Candidates / Ex-Servicemen remain unfilled after shortlisting, those vacancies will be filled from other eligible candidates in the respective category.
  • Rule 371(J) reservation: Posts reserved for Kalyana Karnataka Region are for candidates from that region. Non-371(J) refers to posts from the rest of the state (outside Kalyana Karnataka region).

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 18 years of age as on the last date of application. The following maximum age limits apply:

Sr. No. Category Maximum Age Limit
1 Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Backward Hindus (Category-1) 40 years
2 Other Backward Classes (Categories 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) 38 years
3 All Other Candidates (General/UR) 35 years

Age Relaxation (Over and above the above limits):

  • Ex-Servicemen: Age equivalent to the period of military service rendered (as per Government norms).
  • Differently-Abled (Physically Challenged) Candidates: 10 years additional relaxation.
  • Widowed Women: 10 years additional (candidate must upload a certificate from the Tahsildar confirming widowhood and non-remarriage).

Special Note: As per Government Notification No. ಸಿ.ಆ.ಸು.ಇ:2062:ಸೇನೆನಿ 2025, Bengaluru dated 29.01.2026, all category candidates who were participating in the selection process based on the age schedule as on the notified maximum age limit are to be provided 5 years of one-time age relaxation (As a One Time Measure) for participating in recruitment processes. Candidates who applied under the previous notification dated 06.04.2026 and were within the age limit are considered eligible for the current notification's age limit as on 30.06.2026.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Category Application Fee
General / Other Categories (2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) Rs. 1,000/- + Gateway charges (as applicable)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe / Category-1 / Differently-Abled / Ex-Servicemen Rs. 500/- + Gateway charges (as applicable)

Important Fee Rules:

  • Application and fee payment start date: 01-06-2026
  • Last date for application and fee payment: 30-06-2026
  • Payment mode: Online only - Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, or Google Pay; DD/Postal Order/Money Order/any other mode is NOT accepted.
  • Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Each post requires a separate application and separate fee payment.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

(A) Manager / First Division Assistant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Vacancy filling is done through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting for Written Exam: Online applications are screened based on prescribed educational qualifications and required documents. Eligible candidates are called for written examination conducted by an external government-authorized body. Based on marks scored in the prescribed written examination, an eligibility list is prepared and candidates are called for interview in a 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum to qualify for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85 out of 100 to qualify) combined with interview marks are totaled to prepare a final merit list (Merit List), following reservation norms for final appointment.

Written Examination Pattern for Manager / First Division Assistant - 200 Marks, Duration: 2 Hours, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General English Language 25
3 General Knowledge 25
4 Co-operative Subject 50
5 Indian Constitution 25
6 Banking, Social Economics Activities and Consumer Affairs 25
Total   200

(B) Second Division Assistant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Vacancy filling is done through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Same process as Manager post - online applications screened, eligible candidates called for written exam by external body, merit list prepared in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85 to qualify) combined with interview marks totaled to prepare final Merit List, following reservation norms.

Written Examination Pattern for Second Division Assistant - 200 Marks, Duration: 2 Hours, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General English Language 25
3 General Knowledge 25
4 Co-operative Subject 50
5 Indian Constitution 25
6 Banking, Social Economics Activities and Consumer Affairs 25
Total   200

(C) Vehicle Driver

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Selection is through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Online applications screened based on prescribed qualifications and required documents; eligible candidates called for written exam by external body in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85) combined with interview marks for final Merit List; reservation norms followed.

Written Examination Pattern for Vehicle Driver - 100 Marks, Duration: 1 Hour, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General Knowledge 50
Total   100

(D) Junior Servant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Selection is through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Online applications screened; eligible candidates called for written exam by external body in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85) combined with interview marks for final Merit List; reservation norms followed.

Written Examination Pattern for Junior Servant - 100 Marks, Duration: 1 Hour, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General Knowledge 50
Total   100

Note: Question papers for all posts will be in Kannada and English (Kannada language and General English section excepted). Each question carries 1 mark; there is NO negative marking. Candidates scoring equal marks at written exam will be given preference to older candidates as per Karnataka State Government recruitment rules.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply

Candidates must apply Online only through the Bank's official website. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before filling the application, candidates must mandatorily read the notification and instructions carefully.

Step-by-Step Application Process:

Step 1: Visit the Bank's official website https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in and open the link "BDCC RECRUITMENT 2026" on a desktop/laptop computer. Read the notification and instructions carefully before filling the application.

  • After clicking the registration box, a confirmation check box will appear for candidates who have previously applied and paid the fee. Those candidates must re-submit the application with the same registration number clearly mentioned, upload documents, and re-submit.
  • A check box will appear: "Are you re-applying after already submitting an application and fee payment?" If Yes, mention the previous application registration number clearly, upload documents, and re-submit the application.

Step 2: Visit http://www.bellarydcc.bank.in, register and log in. Enter your SSLC marks, name, date of birth, disability status, and other required details. Verify mobile number and email ID via OTP. Register successfully.

  • Candidates who previously applied and downloaded the application under the earlier notification must register again with a new registration number and re-upload documents per the new requirements. Fee once paid for the earlier application is not refunded, and no separate fee is required again for that post.

Step 3: Use your registration number and date of birth as login credentials. Review and edit all details and qualification information if needed. Preview the application carefully before confirming. Once submitted, no information can be changed.

Step 4: Upload Photo and Signature. The candidate must upload their passport-size photograph on a blank white paper with signature below using a black Sketch/Marker pen; scan and upload. Applications without photo/signature or incomplete applications will be rejected.

  • After uploading photo and signature, upload all required SSLC marks/reservation-related documents with self-attestation, then submit.

Step 5: After submitting, pay the application fee and applicable gateway charges through the Payment Gateway link.

Photo Specifications:

  • File size: Less than 100 KB
  • DPI Setting: 100 DPI
  • Quality: 100%
  • Color: Color
  • Format: JPG/JPEG
  • Photo size: 35mm × 45mm; Signature below: 35mm × 20mm

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Required Documents

The following documents must be uploaded online at the time of application and must be valid as on the last date of application. Originals must be produced at the time of verification.

For All Posts:

  1. Educational qualification certificates or mark sheets for the prescribed qualification (all years/semesters).
  2. SSLC marks card / School attendance certificate or Cumulative Record Extract (for date of birth proof).
  3. Category/reservation certificate as applicable.

For Reservation / Age Relaxation Certificates:

  1. Ex-Servicemen: Discharge certificate and Pension/Gratuity certificate from the authorized authority.
  2. SC/ST (Category A, B, C) / ST-Pangada candidates: Category certificates in the prescribed Government format - SC/ST in Form-D/E/F as applicable; Category-1 candidates in Form-E; 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates in Form-F issued by concerned Tahsildar.
  3. Candidates currently employed: Service certificate from employer and No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the relevant recruitment authority.
  4. Kalyana Karnataka Region candidates: Eligibility proof certificate as per Annexure-2 from the authorized sub-divisional authority.
  5. Rural candidates: Certificate in Form-1 and General candidates in Form-1 and Form-2 from the concerned Tahsildar.
  6. Differently-Abled candidates: Medical certificate from the District-level Medical Board in the prescribed Government format.
  7. Manager / First Division Assistant post: All year/semester mark sheets of the degree.
  8. Second Division Assistant post: Second PUC mark sheets / current student proof.
  9. Vehicle Driver post: SSLC marks card and valid legal driving license.
  10. Junior Servant post: SSLC marks card.
  11. Candidates applying for any post must mandatorily upload a self-portrait passport-size photograph with signature as described above.
  12. Candidates currently working anywhere must upload service certificate from the head of that organization and a No Objection Certificate.
  13. Rural / Ex-Servicemen / Kannada Medium / Differently-Abled category claimants must upload the competent authority-issued certificate.

Special Instructions for Previously Applied Candidates

  • Candidates who had applied and paid the fee under Bank Recruitment Notification No. 12:2026–27 dated 06.04.2026 and who wish to apply for the same posts in the current notification must mandatorily re-apply online. They must clearly mention the previous registration number and re-upload all required documents. No separate fee payment is required for those posts.

  • Candidates who had applied and downloaded the application under the 06.04.2026 notification must register freshly with a new registration number, re-submit, and upload documents as per current requirements. Once Application Re-submit is done, it cannot be re-edited and fees paid will not be refunded. Each post still requires a separate application; one Application Re-submit per post is allowed.
  • Information regarding re-application will be communicated via SMS and email to the registered mobile number and email ID of candidates who had previously applied and downloaded their application under the earlier notification.

Caste Reservation Certificate Requirements

For candidates claiming reservation benefits:

  • SC (Category A, B, C) / ST / Pangada candidates: Upload certificate in Form-D
  • Category-1 candidates: Upload certificate in Form-E
  • Category-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates: Upload certificate in Form-F, obtained from the concerned Tahsildar; these certificates for Backward Hindu categories must be valid within 5 years from the date of application.

Rural Candidate Reservation:

  • Rural candidates must have studied from Class 1 to Class 10 in a village (rural area) under the current rules; certificate must be current and valid.
  • SC/ST/Pangada candidates claiming Rural reservation must submit Form-2 rural reservation certificate.
  • General category candidates claiming rural reservation must submit Form-1 rural certificate along with proof that they are not otherwise listed as urban.
  • General category, SC/ST/Pangada, Category-1, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates who fail to submit the required rural reservation certificate will be treated as ineligible for rural reservation.
  • Note: Certificate in Form-2 must be obtained from the Headmaster of the school where the candidate studied and attested by the concerned field education officer.

Kannada Medium Candidates:

  • Candidates claiming Kannada Medium reservation must submit a certificate from the Headmaster of the school where they studied Kannada medium along with date of school's issue, signed certificate, and must be held valid as on the last date of application. Original copies must be produced at verification. If not produced, the Kannada Medium reservation will be cancelled.

Scheme-Deprived (Yojane Nirashritha) Candidates:

  • Must produce a certificate from the concerned Tahsildar in the prescribed format as per current rules. Certificate must be valid up to the last date of application and must be current.

Ex-Servicemen:

  • Ex-Serviceman means a person who has served in any rank (combatant or non-combatant) in the Central Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) - excluding persons who served in Defence Security Corps, General Reserve Engineering Force, Lok Sahayak Sena, and Para-Military Forces.
  • Family members (spouse/husband/wife and children, including adopted children) of persons who died while serving in Central Armed Forces are eligible for Ex-Servicemen reservation; however, age relaxation will not be provided to them.
  • Eligible cases for Ex-Servicemen reservation:
    • Retired voluntarily from such service after receiving pension; OR
    • Released from military service on medical grounds with medical and physical disability pension; OR
    • Released from service due to reduction of personnel; OR
    • Released from service after completing specific service and receiving gratuity, after fulfilling period of service - including: persons retired from continuous service, persons with physical disability due to military service, and gazette prize winners.
  • Persons currently re-employed in government service after retiring from military service must apply for military reservation through their current service NOC before appointment; they are allowed to enjoy all benefits available to ex-servicemen during re-employment, but the bank will not be responsible if they are not matched with the post. They must submit their discharge certificate before applying.
  • Ex-servicemen applicants, persons dependent on ex-servicemen who were killed or permanently disabled while in service - (spouse/husband/wife and children, including adopted children) - are eligible for Ex-Servicemen reservation benefits. Age relaxation will not be provided to dependents. They must submit the discharge certificate and permanence/gratuity certificate; at the time of original document verification, original copies must be produced.

Differently-Abled (Physically Challenged) Candidates:

  • Only candidates with a minimum of 40% benchmark disability certified by the District-level Medical Board in the prescribed Government format (NOT in any other format) are eligible for differently-abled reservation. Certificate must be issued in the prescribed Government notification format.
  • Differently-Abled candidates who require a scribe for the written examination must upload a special medical certificate from the Medical Board stating that they are unable to write due to physical disability.
  • The scribe requirement must be indicated at the time of application in the specified field in the online application; only when required, the candidate may request a scribe from the examination centre authority.

Who Should NOT Apply for Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not meet the prescribed educational qualifications (degree for Manager/FDA; Second PUC for SDA; SSLC for Driver and Junior Servant) as on the last date of application.

  • Candidates who cannot read, write, speak, and understand Kannada - knowledge of Kannada is mandatory for all posts.
  • Candidates below 18 years of age or above the maximum age limit applicable to their category.
  • OBC (Category-2A/2B/3A/3B) Creamy Layer candidates applying under reserved categories - only non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates are eligible for reservation.
  • Candidates with less than 40% disability who apply for Differently-Abled reservation - only 40%+ benchmark disability qualifies.
  • Vehicle Driver candidates who do not hold a valid legal driving license.
  • Candidates currently employed in any organization who cannot produce an NOC from their employer.
  • Candidates who obtained degrees or certificates from institutions declared fake or derecognized by competent educational authorities - such applications will be rejected at any stage.
  • Candidates who provide false information or suppress facts - such candidates are liable for criminal prosecution and disqualification at any stage of the selection process.
  • Persons who are members of or active in any banned organization, or who have attempted to overthrow the Government of India, or who have been convicted under any law, or who have taken part in activities against the sovereignty or integrity of India, or who have been dismissed from any government service.
  • Candidates with mental illness or mental disability.
  • A male candidate who has more than one living wife, and a woman candidate who has married a man who already has another living wife, are not eligible.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying for the wrong post for which you do not meet the educational qualification.

  • Selecting the wrong category (General/SC/ST/OBC/EWS) - categories cannot be changed after submission; mismatched certificates lead to cancellation.
  • OBC (Backward Hindu 2A/2B/3A/3B) candidates from Creamy Layer applying under OBC reservation instead of General category.
  • Not uploading all mandatory documents at the time of online application - incomplete applications will be rejected outright.
  • Uploading blurred, illegible, or incorrect photographs, signatures, or certificates - leading to rejection at document verification stage.
  • Using an inactive or wrong mobile number and email ID - exam dates, interview schedules, and admit cards are communicated only through SMS and email.
  • Submitting multiple applications for the same post without following the proper Re-submit process - only the last valid application is considered and fees for others are forfeited.
  • Not reading the separate instructions for candidates who applied under the earlier notification (06.04.2026) - they must re-apply with their old registration number and need not pay fee again for the same post.
  • Not noting that each post requires a separate application and separate fee - applying for multiple posts with a single application/fee is not permitted.
  • Paying the fee through DD/Postal Order/Money Order or any offline mode - only online payment (Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card/UPI/Google Pay) is accepted.
  • Not keeping originals of all uploaded documents ready for verification - originals must be produced at the time of interview/verification.
  • Ignoring the minimum qualifying mark requirement: candidates must score a minimum of 85 out of 100 (or 200) in the written examination to qualify for the interview.

Important Instructions for Bellary DCC Bank  Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Applications must be submitted Online only at https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in; no other mode is accepted.

  • Candidates must mandatorily keep Kannada language proficiency - it is essential for all posts.
  • Information provided by candidates at the time of application regarding category, date of birth, educational qualification, age relaxation, etc., can only be for consideration purposes and will not be changed later; no request for change will be entertained afterward.
  • Incomplete applications, applications without photo/signature, or applications with incorrect information will be summarily rejected.
  • Candidates must self-attest all documents and mandatorily upload them online.
  • Candidates must keep a printout of the submitted application for their own reference.
  • Admit cards for the written examination and date/venue/time of interview will be communicated via SMS, email, or the Bank's website; admit cards will NOT be sent by post.
  • The Bank/external body reserves the right to advance, postpone, cancel, and change the rules of the recruitment process at any time without giving any reason.
  • The decision of the Bank's Board/Staff Recruitment Committee shall be final in all matters relating to recruitment; no correspondence will be entertained.
  • Candidates must serve a probation period of 2 years after selection.
  • Selected candidates will be provided security deposit and personal bond as informed by the Bank; they must sign a contract and pay the prescribed security deposit on joining.
  • This recruitment is subject to directions and orders of the Karnataka Government / High Court during the recruitment notification period.
  • Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification.
  • No correspondence regarding recruitment will be entertained from any candidate after the process has concluded.

RRB NTPC Exam City Intimation Slip 2026 Out - Download NTPC Exam City Slip

Railway Recruitment Board as released the City Intimation Slip 2026 for NTPC examination on June 3, 2026. Candidates appearing for Railway Recruitment Board NTPC exam can check their exam city slip from the official website https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in. The city intimation slip contains crucial information about exam city, date, shift timing, and reporting time. This document helps candidates plan their travel and accommodation arrangements before the admit card release. In this article, we provide the direct download link (once live), step-by-step instructions, and all important details about Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026.

  • City Slip Status: Released
  • Exam Dates: June 13, 2026 to June 20, 2026 (CBT-1 Phase 2)
  • Official Website: https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in
  • Download Available For: Exams scheduled till June 20, 2026
  • Admit Card Release: 4 days before exam date
  • Total Vacancies: 11,597 Posts (NTPC Undergraduate)

What is Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026?

The Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026 is an advance notification document released before the admit card. It provides candidates with essential preliminary information about their examination to help them make necessary travel and accommodation arrangements. The city slip is NOT your admit card but serves as an important reference document.

Key Information Available in City Slip:

  • Allotted exam city and state
  • Scheduled exam date
  • Shift timing (Morning/Afternoon/Evening)
  • Reporting time at exam center
  • Gate closing time
  • Candidate's name and registration number
  • Category (SC/ST/OBC/General/EWS)
  • PwBD status (if applicable)

Important Notice: The city intimation slip is NOT your admit card and does not permit you to appear in the exam. It is only for informational purposes. You must download the admit card separately (available 4 days before exam) to enter the examination hall.

How to Download Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026? (Step-by-Step Guide)

Step 1: Visit Official Website

  • Open your web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Edge recommended)
  • Visit Railway Recruitment Board official website: https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in
  • Or use your regional RRB website if applicable
  • Ensure you have stable internet connection
  • On the homepage, scroll down to "Latest Updates" or "Important Links" section
  • Look for link: "Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026" or "Download City Slip for NTPC CEN 07/2025"
  • Click on the city intimation slip download link
  • You may be redirected to a candidate login page
  • Enter your Registration Number or Application Number
  • Enter your Date of Birth in DD/MM/YYYY format OR Password
  • Type the Captcha Code as shown in the image (case-sensitive)
  • Double-check all entered information for accuracy
  • Click on the "Submit" or "Login" button
  • Your city intimation slip will appear on the screen
  • Carefully verify all details displayed
  • Check: Name, Registration No., Exam City, Date, Shift Timing
  • Click on "Download" or "Save as PDF" button
  • Save the file to your computer/mobile device
  • Take a printout of the city slip for reference
  • Save a backup copy in cloud storage (Google Drive/Email)
  • Keep both digital and physical copies safe

Download your city intimation slip as soon as it is released to avoid server overload and last-minute technical issues. The website may experience heavy traffic during peak hours.

Details Available in Railway Recruitment Board City Intimation Slip 2026

When you download your city intimation slip, you will find the following information:

Information Type Details
Candidate Name Full name as per application form
Registration Number Unique application/registration ID
Exam Name NTPC Undergraduate Examination 2026
Exam City/State Name of city and state where exam center is located
Exam Date Scheduled date of your examination
Shift Timing Morning Shift / Afternoon Shift / Evening Shift
Reporting Time Time to reach the exam center
Gate Closing Time Last entry time at exam center gate
Exam Duration 90 minutes (120 minutes for PwBD candidates)
Category SC/ST/OBC/General/EWS
PwBD Status Yes/No (if applicable)
Important Instructions Dos and Don'ts for exam day

Note: The exact exam center address will be mentioned only in the admit card, which will be released 4 days before your scheduled exam date. The city slip only mentions the exam city name.
